title: ハウス野菜栽培者
rationale: While core tasks like planting and harvesting require physical presence and manual dexterity, greenhouse farming in Japan is increasingly data-driven and automated. AI significantly impacts the management side—optimizing environmental controls, predicting yields, and analyzing market trends—but the physical labor and real-time biological monitoring maintain a need for human intervention.

title: ブリーダー
rationale: The core of a breeder's work involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time biological monitoring, such as cleaning cages, assisting in births, and observing animal behavior. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like genetic data analysis for breeding pairs, health monitoring via sensors, and customer communication, the fundamental requirement for physical presence and 'hands-on' animal care remains high.

title: 動物園飼育員
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor, sensory observation of live animals, and manual tasks like cleaning and feeding in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with health monitoring via sensors, automated feeding schedules, and administrative record-keeping, the physical presence and human judgment required for animal welfare and public interaction remain essential.

title: 厩舎スタッフ
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual horse care, and high-level equestrian skills in an unpredictable environment with live animals. While AI may assist in health monitoring via sensors or optimizing training schedules, the fundamental tasks of mucking stalls, grooming, and physical racehorse training cannot be automated by current or near-future AI technology.

title: 林業作業
rationale: Forestry is a highly physical occupation conducted in unpredictable, rugged natural environments that are difficult for current AI and robotics to navigate. While AI may improve peripheral tasks like forest mapping via drones or optimizing timber logistics, the core work of planting, thinning, and harvesting on steep slopes remains dependent on human physical labor and manual machine operation.

title: 果樹栽培者
rationale: The core tasks of fruit cultivation—such as thinning, bagging, and harvesting—require high manual dexterity and physical presence in unpredictable outdoor environments, which are difficult to automate fully. However, AI will increasingly assist in peripheral areas like yield prediction, pest detection via computer vision, and optimized sales/marketing strategies, especially as Japan's labor shortage drives the adoption of smart agriculture.

title: 植物工場の栽培管理
rationale: The core of this job involves monitoring environmental data (light, CO2, nutrients) and following manuals, tasks that are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automated climate control systems. While physical tasks like harvesting and cleaning remain, Japan's labor shortage is driving rapid integration of AI with robotics in these controlled environments, shifting the human role toward high-level oversight.

title: 水産養殖従事者
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor in unpredictable marine environments, such as cleaning nets, handling livestock, and responding to weather emergencies, which are resistant to AI. While AI and IoT are increasingly used for automated feeding, water quality monitoring, and health diagnostics to address Japan's labor shortages, these tools serve as peripheral aids rather than replacements for the essential physical presence and manual intervention required.

title: 沿岸漁業従事者
rationale: Coastal fishing is a physical, hands-on occupation conducted in unpredictable marine environments that require real-time human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like weather forecasting or sonar data analysis, the core work of handling nets, sorting catch, and navigating small vessels remains highly resistant to automation.

title: 稲作農業者
rationale: While core tasks like planting and harvesting are physical and already highly mechanized, AI is increasingly used for precision agriculture, such as drone-based pest detection and automated water management. However, the physical nature of the work and the need for real-time human judgment regarding weather and soil conditions in an unpredictable outdoor environment limit full AI replacement.

title: 花き栽培者
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and sensory judgment in a biological environment, which are difficult to automate. While AI and IoT significantly enhance environmental control (temperature, irrigation) and market trend analysis, the physical tasks of planting, pruning, and harvesting remain human-centric in the medium term.

title: 造園工
rationale: Landscape gardening is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, spatial reasoning, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI can assist in the design and planning phases (CAD/3D modeling), the core tasks of planting, pruning, and stone arrangement remain resistant to automation due to the unique biological variability of plants and the physical complexity of garden sites.

title: 酪農従事者
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor, animal handling, and maintenance of facilities in an unpredictable biological environment, which limits full AI replacement. While AI and IoT (milking robots, health monitoring sensors) are significantly improving productivity and automating data management, the physical presence required for animal welfare and equipment troubleshooting remains essential.

title: IR広報担当
rationale: IR involves heavy digital output such as annual reports, press releases, and financial analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and drafting. While high-level stakeholder relationship management and strategic communication with executives provide a buffer, AI will significantly reduce the headcount needed for data synthesis and content production in Japan's corporate sector.

title: NPO法人職員（企画・運営）
rationale: The core tasks of planning, research, grant writing, and impact analysis are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime candidates for AI-assisted productivity gains. While the role requires high-level human coordination with stakeholders and deep empathy for social issues, the administrative and strategic documentation that consumes much of an NPO worker's time in Japan is highly exposed to AI automation.

title: Webマーケティング（ネット広告・販売促進）
rationale: This occupation is entirely digital and data-driven, involving tasks like data analysis, ad copywriting, and market research that are core strengths of modern AI. Generative AI and automated bidding algorithms are already taking over content creation and campaign optimization, significantly reducing the manual labor required for routine marketing operations.

title: アニメ制作進行管理
rationale: While the core of the role involves interpersonal coordination and 'soft' management, the underlying tasks—scheduling, budget tracking, and asset management—are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. As the actual production of animation (drawing, coloring, and background art) increasingly shifts toward AI-assisted workflows, the production manager's role will transform from manual tracking and physical asset transport to overseeing highly digital, AI-integrated pipelines.

title: イベントの企画・運営
rationale: AI significantly enhances the digital aspects of this role, such as generating event concepts, marketing copy, and managing complex logistics or schedules. However, the core of the job requires high-level stakeholder negotiation, real-time crisis management at physical venues, and leadership of human staff, which remain difficult to automate.

title: キッティング作業員（PCセットアップ作業員）
rationale: While the software configuration and cloning aspects are increasingly automated through MDM (Mobile Device Management) and AI-driven deployment tools, the core job still requires significant physical labor including unboxing, cabling, and hardware labeling. In Japan, the high demand for physical security and 'on-site' verification creates a barrier to full automation, though AI will continue to simplify the troubleshooting and manual documentation parts of the role.

title: コンプライアンス推進担当
rationale: This role is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of regulations, contract checking, and the creation of manuals—tasks where LLMs and specialized legal AI excel. While it requires human judgment and internal communication to navigate corporate culture, AI can automate the vast majority of the research, drafting, and monitoring functions, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the headcount needed for routine compliance checks.

title: コールセンターオペレーター
rationale: This occupation is highly digital and involves routine information processing, making it prime for AI disruption through LLM-powered chatbots and voice synthesis. While complex emotional escalations still require humans, Japan's severe labor shortage is aggressively driving the adoption of AI to automate both inbound inquiries and outbound telemarketing.

title: タクシー配車オペレーター
rationale: This occupation is highly susceptible to AI because its core tasks—receiving location data, matching it with available vehicles, and optimizing routes—are fundamentally information-processing tasks that are already being automated by AI-driven dispatch algorithms and mobile apps (like GO or S.RIDE). While some human intervention remains for complex customer service or emergency handling, the rapid integration of GPS, voice recognition, and automated dispatch systems in Japan is significantly reducing the need for human operators.

title: ディスパッチャー（航空機運航管理者）
rationale: The core tasks of analyzing meteorological data, calculating fuel loads, and optimizing flight paths are highly digital and data-driven, making them ideal for AI optimization. While the role requires a national license and involves critical human-in-the-loop decision-making for safety and legal accountability, AI is rapidly shifting the job from manual calculation to high-level system monitoring and exception management.

title: データ入力
rationale: This occupation involves routine, digital information processing that is highly susceptible to automation through OCR, LLMs, and RPA. AI can now transcribe handwritten text and structured data with high accuracy, eliminating the core need for manual keyboard entry and human verification in most scenarios.

title: ネット通販の企画開発
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving market analysis, product planning, and digital marketing—all areas where Generative AI excels. AI can automate data-driven trend analysis, generate marketing copy, and optimize sales strategies, significantly increasing the productivity of individual planners and potentially reducing the total headcount needed for these roles in Japan's growing EC market.

title: ネット通販の運営
rationale: The core tasks of e-commerce operations—inventory management, order processing, data entry, and customer support—are predominantly digital and highly structured, making them prime targets for AI automation. Generative AI is already significantly impacting product description writing and customer inquiry handling, while predictive analytics are taking over demand forecasting and stock management in the Japanese market.

title: パラリーガル（弁護士補助職）
rationale: The core tasks of a paralegal—legal research, case law analysis, document drafting, and evidence organization—are fundamentally digital and text-based, making them highly susceptible to Large Language Models (LLMs). While Japan-specific legal procedures and interpersonal coordination provide a small buffer, AI can already automate the vast majority of their information-processing workload, leading to significant role restructuring.

title: フランチャイズチェーン・スーパーバイザー
rationale: The core tasks of data analysis, sales forecasting, and improvement planning are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization, significantly increasing individual productivity. While the role requires physical site visits and high-level interpersonal negotiation with franchise owners, the shift toward AI-generated business insights will transform the supervisor's role from a data messenger to a strategic consultant.

title: プロスポーツ運営団体職員（企画・運営）
rationale: The core tasks of marketing, ticket sales analysis, fan club management, and digital promotion are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While physical event coordination and local community negotiations provide a buffer, AI will significantly reshape how these professionals analyze fan data, generate promotional content, and manage customer relations.

title: マンション管理フロント
rationale: A significant portion of the workload involves routine digital tasks such as drafting meeting minutes, creating financial reports, and analyzing maintenance data, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role remains anchored by the need for physical site inspections and complex interpersonal mediation between residents, which limits full replacement in the near term.

title: マーケティング・リサーチャー
rationale: The core tasks of marketing researchers—survey design, data collection, statistical analysis, and report generation—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with AI capabilities. LLMs and specialized AI tools can now automate complex data synthesis, sentiment analysis, and even the drafting of strategic recommendations, significantly reducing the human labor required for each project.

title: 一般事務
rationale: General office work (一般事務) consists primarily of routine digital tasks such as data entry, document creation, and schedule management, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and RPA. While minor physical tasks like tea serving or mail sorting remain, the core value proposition is rapidly being replaced by AI tools that can process information and handle communications more efficiently than humans.

title: 人事事務
rationale: HR administrative work in Japan is highly digital and involves routine information processing, such as payroll calculation, social insurance procedures, and attendance management, which are prime targets for AI and HRTech automation. While high-level labor negotiations and complex employee relations still require human judgment, the vast majority of the core tasks described—data entry, document preparation, and regulatory compliance—are being rapidly restructured by AI-driven productivity gains.

title: 介護事務
rationale: The core of this role involves routine digital tasks such as medical billing (recept creation), data entry, and document preparation, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and integration with existing software. While there are interpersonal elements like reception and phone handling, the primary output is administrative and digital, making it highly exposed to productivity gains that could reduce the total number of workers needed.

title: 企業法務担当
rationale: Corporate legal work is predominantly digital and information-based, involving contract drafting, legal research, and compliance monitoring—all areas where LLMs and specialized LegalTech AI excel. While 'strategic legal affairs' and complex negotiations still require human judgment and organizational context, the vast majority of routine documentation and analysis can be significantly automated or enhanced by AI, leading to major productivity shifts in Japanese white-collar legal departments.

title: 企画・調査担当
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving market analysis, consumer trend forecasting, and strategic planning—all areas where LLMs and AI analytics excel. While final decision-making and cross-departmental negotiation require human judgment, AI can now automate the bulk of data synthesis, report drafting, and framework generation, significantly reducing the headcount needed for these functions.

title: 入国審査官
rationale: The core tasks of verifying documents, cross-referencing databases, and assessing eligibility are highly digital and data-driven, making them susceptible to AI automation (e.g., facial recognition and automated gates already in use). While complex cases involving human judgment, refugee status, and legal nuance require human oversight, the vast majority of routine processing is being streamlined by AI to handle Japan's increasing traveler volume with fewer personnel.

title: 内部監査人
rationale: Internal auditing is a knowledge-intensive digital occupation involving data analysis, document review, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains. While AI can significantly streamline risk assessment and anomaly detection, the role still requires human judgment, ethical oversight, and high-level communication with management to drive organizational change.

title: 出荷・受荷事務
rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—inventory management, data entry, and document reconciliation—are fundamentally digital information processing tasks already integrated with WMS and ERP systems. While some physical verification and coordination remain, AI and computer vision are rapidly automating visual inspections and demand forecasting, significantly reducing the need for manual administrative oversight in Japanese logistics hubs.

title: 医療事務
rationale: The core tasks of medical office work—calculating medical fees, creating insurance claims (recepts), and checking for errors—are highly structured digital processes that are already being automated by AI and specialized software. While front-desk patient interaction remains, the back-office administrative functions are extremely susceptible to full automation as Japan's healthcare system continues to digitize and integrate AI-driven auditing tools.

title: 受付事務
rationale: The core tasks of verifying appointments, contacting internal staff, and providing directions are highly routine and digitalizable. Many Japanese companies are already replacing physical reception desks with automated touch-panel systems and AI-driven chatbots, though a small human element remains for high-level VIP hospitality and security monitoring.

title: 商品企画開発（チェーンストア）
rationale: This role involves heavy data analysis, market trend forecasting, and the creation of planning documents, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. While it requires physical site visits and human negotiation with suppliers, the core output—product strategy and planning—is increasingly being reshaped by AI's ability to process consumer behavior data and optimize supply chains.

title: 営業事務
rationale: This role consists almost entirely of digital information processing, such as creating quotes, managing sales data, and responding to emails, which are tasks AI can now automate with high accuracy. While it involves some human-to-human coordination, Japan's labor shortage and the widespread use of ERP/CRM systems are rapidly shifting these routine administrative duties toward AI-driven automation and self-service portals.

title: 国家公務員（行政事務）
rationale: The core tasks of national civil servants—drafting policy, analyzing statistics, preparing Diet responses, and processing permits—are fundamentally information-based and digital. While high-level political judgment and inter-agency negotiations provide a human buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document drafting, and routine administrative processing that currently consume a vast majority of their labor hours.

title: 国際公務員
rationale: International civil servants perform high-level knowledge work including policy analysis, report writing, and cross-lingual communication, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While diplomatic negotiations and field-based humanitarian work require human presence and cultural nuance, the core administrative, research, and linguistic tasks are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.

title: 地方公務員（行政事務）
rationale: A vast majority of local government administrative work involves routine document creation, data processing, and regulatory interpretation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While face-to-face resident services and complex local policy-making provide a buffer, Japan's severe labor shortages and digital transformation (DX) initiatives are aggressively pushing AI adoption to maintain public services with fewer staff.

title: 外務公務員（外交官）
rationale: Diplomats perform high-level knowledge work including information analysis, policy formulation, and drafting complex documents, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the core of the role involves sensitive human-to-human negotiation, building trust-based relationships, and high-stakes judgment in unpredictable geopolitical environments, which provides a significant buffer against full automation.

title: 太陽光発電の企画・調査
rationale: The core tasks of data analysis, site feasibility studies, and financial modeling for solar proposals are highly digital and increasingly automated by AI and GIS tools. While physical site visits and stakeholder negotiations provide a buffer, the shift toward 'self-consumption' models requires complex energy-use simulations that AI can perform more efficiently than humans.

title: 学校事務
rationale: The core tasks of school administration—including payroll, budget management, certificate issuance, and student records—are digital information-processing tasks highly susceptible to AI automation. While some face-to-face interaction with students and parents remains, Japan's push for 'GIGA School' digitalization and administrative efficiency in response to labor shortages will likely lead to significant restructuring of these roles.

title: 学校事務（私立大学）
rationale: The role involves extensive digital information processing, including document creation, scheduling, and data management, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While student counseling and inter-departmental negotiations require human empathy and judgment, Japan's shrinking student population is driving universities to aggressively adopt AI for administrative efficiency and marketing automation.

title: 広報・PR担当
rationale: A significant portion of PR work involves digital content creation, such as writing press releases, social media posts, and internal newsletters, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While crisis management and high-level relationship building with media and stakeholders still require human judgment and emotional intelligence, AI tools for sentiment analysis and data-driven strategy are rapidly increasing individual productivity, likely leading to leaner PR teams in Japan's corporate sector.

title: 損害保険事務
rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving document processing, damage assessment, and legal/medical data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While complex negotiations and empathetic human interaction remain, AI is already being integrated into Japan's insurance sector for automated claims processing, voice-to-text intake, and fraud detection, significantly reducing the human labor required per case.

title: 旅行会社カウンター係
rationale: The core tasks of information retrieval, itinerary planning, and booking are increasingly handled by AI agents that can process vast datasets more efficiently than humans. While the 'face-to-face' reassurance remains a factor in Japan, the shift toward digital self-service and AI-driven personalization significantly reduces the need for human counter staff.

title: 検針員
rationale: The core task of manual meter reading is being rapidly rendered obsolete by the nationwide rollout of smart meters (IoT), which automate data collection entirely. While some physical presence remains for older infrastructure or difficult-to-access locations, Japan's utility sectors are aggressively transitioning to digital monitoring to combat labor shortages, leading to a near-total restructuring of this occupation.

title: 生産・工程管理事務
rationale: This occupation consists primarily of routine digital tasks such as data entry, production scheduling, and progress tracking, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While it requires some interpersonal coordination and negotiation, the core functions of analyzing production data and managing supply chains are increasingly being handled by sophisticated AI-integrated ERP systems.

title: 秘書
rationale: The core tasks of a secretary—scheduling, email filtering, document preparation, and information synthesis—are highly digital and increasingly automated by LLM-based agents. While high-level executive assistants still require human nuance and physical hospitality, the vast majority of administrative and organizational functions in Japan's corporate sector are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and displacement.

title: 税務事務官
rationale: The core tasks of reviewing tax returns, managing data, and identifying audit targets are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and predictive analytics, which the National Tax Agency is already actively promoting. While field audits, enforcement (seizures), and complex international negotiations require human authority and physical presence, the vast majority of the administrative and analytical workload is digital and ripe for restructuring.

title: 総務事務
rationale: General affairs (総務事務) involves high volumes of routine digital tasks such as document management, scheduling, and data entry, which are highly susceptible to AI and RPA automation. While it requires some physical coordination and interpersonal communication for events and facility management, the core administrative functions are being rapidly restructured by AI-driven productivity tools and cloud-based systems.

title: 臨床開発モニター
rationale: The core tasks of a CRA—monitoring data integrity, reviewing Case Report Forms (CRF), and ensuring regulatory compliance—are highly digital and data-centric, making them prime targets for AI-driven automation and remote monitoring technologies. While human negotiation with medical institutions and doctors remains essential, AI will significantly streamline document review, anomaly detection in clinical data, and report generation, drastically increasing individual productivity and reducing the total headcount required for monitoring.

title: 診療情報管理士
rationale: The core tasks of this role—medical coding (ICD-10), data entry, and clinical record auditing—are entirely digital and based on structured information processing, making them highly susceptible to LLM-based automation. While human oversight remains necessary for legal compliance and complex cases, AI can now perform high-accuracy coding and data analysis, significantly reducing the human labor required per hospital.

title: 調剤薬局事務
rationale: The core tasks of data entry into specialized software (rece-com) and the generation of medical insurance claims (receipts) are highly structured digital processes that AI can largely automate. While there is a physical customer service component, Japan's push for digital health records and automated pharmacy systems significantly reduces the need for manual administrative labor in this role.

title: 貿易事務
rationale: Trade clerks (貿易事務) perform tasks that are almost entirely digital, involving document preparation, data entry, and regulatory research which are highly susceptible to AI automation. Large Language Models (LLMs) and specialized trade platforms are rapidly automating translation, legal compliance checks, and logistics coordination, significantly reducing the need for manual administrative labor in this sector.

title: 通信販売受付事務
rationale: This occupation consists almost entirely of routine information processing and digital communication, tasks where LLMs and automated systems already excel. While some human empathy is needed for complex complaints, the core functions of order entry, basic inquiries, and data management are being rapidly replaced by AI-driven chatbots and automated voice systems in Japan's labor-short market.

title: 郵便局郵便窓口業務
rationale: While the job requires a physical presence for handling parcels and stamps, the core tasks of calculating postage, verifying regulations, and processing transactions are highly susceptible to AI automation via self-service kiosks and AI-powered customer service interfaces. Japan's labor shortage is already driving the adoption of automated counters and digital transformation (DX) in post offices, shifting the human role toward troubleshooting and assisting elderly customers rather than routine processing.

title: 鉄道運転計画・運行管理
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally based on complex data analysis, optimization, and digital monitoring, all of which are core strengths of modern AI and machine learning. While human judgment remains critical for emergency response and high-level strategy, the primary tasks of demand forecasting, schedule optimization (dia-kusei), and real-time disruption management are increasingly being automated by sophisticated algorithms and AI-driven decision support systems.

title: 銀行等窓口事務
rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—routine transaction processing, data entry, and basic financial calculations—are digital and increasingly automated via AI and self-service kiosks. While high-level consulting and fraud prevention require human judgment, Japan's shrinking branch networks and the rapid shift toward digital banking are significantly reducing the need for traditional human-mediated window services.

title: 風力発電の企画・調査
rationale: The role involves significant digital knowledge work such as data analysis (wind patterns), financial modeling, and legal research, which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires extensive physical site visits, complex multi-stakeholder negotiations with local residents and governments, and navigating Japan's specific regulatory landscape, which provide a strong human-centric buffer.

title: 駅務員
rationale: While ticketing and fare adjustment are increasingly automated by AI and digital systems, the role retains significant physical and interpersonal responsibilities such as passenger safety, emergency response, and assistance for disabled travelers. Japan's labor shortage is driving more automation in stations, but the need for human presence to manage crowd control and unpredictable social interactions limits full exposure.

title: サッシ取付
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical, requiring manual dexterity and real-time adjustments in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or site measurements, the core work of welding, fitting, and physical installation remains highly resistant to automation in the near decade.

title: タイル工
rationale: Tile setting is a highly physical, manual trade requiring precise spatial judgment and dexterity in unpredictable, non-standardized construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or initial layout designs, the core work of applying mortar and hand-placing tiles remains resistant to digital automation.

title: ブロック積み
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity, heavy lifting, and real-time adjustments in unpredictable outdoor environments. While pre-fabricated units offer some industrial automation, the core tasks of laying blocks, mixing mortar, and finishing joints remain highly resistant to AI and digital replacement.

title: 保温工事
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical, requiring manual dexterity to cut and install insulation materials in unpredictable on-site environments like construction sites and factories. While AI may slightly improve peripheral tasks like blueprint reading or scheduling, the core work of hand-fitting materials to complex piping and machinery remains highly resistant to automation.

title: 内装工
rationale: Interior finishing is a highly physical and manual occupation requiring precise motor skills and real-time adaptation to irregular physical spaces. While AI may assist with administrative tasks, material estimation, or design visualization, the core work of installing floors, wallpaper, and ceilings remains resistant to automation due to the complexity of physical manipulation in diverse on-site environments.

title: 型枠大工
rationale: Formwork carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, spatial reasoning, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable outdoor construction environments. While AI may assist in generating 3D models or optimizing material logistics, the core tasks of assembling, securing, and dismantling heavy physical panels remain resistant to automation by current or near-future AI technologies.

title: 大工
rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity and real-time adaptation to unpredictable construction site environments, which are difficult for AI to automate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like cost estimation, scheduling, and architectural planning (especially for those who run their own businesses), the core labor of cutting, assembling, and installing materials remains firmly human-centric.

title: 左官
rationale: Plastering (Sakan) is a highly skilled manual trade requiring precise physical coordination, tactile feedback, and real-time adaptation to material consistency and environmental conditions. While AI may assist in peripheral business management or material science, the core task of hand-finishing walls in unpredictable construction environments remains extremely difficult to automate with current or near-future robotics.

title: 建設・土木作業員
rationale: The core tasks involve manual labor in unpredictable, non-standardized outdoor environments that require physical dexterity and real-time human judgment. While AI may optimize logistics or scheduling at the management level, the physical nature of digging, hauling, and site preparation remains highly resistant to AI replacement in the next decade.

title: 舗装工
rationale: Paving work is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that requires manual labor and the operation of heavy machinery. While AI may slightly optimize logistics or autonomous machinery in the long term, the core tasks of physical installation, site adjustment, and manual finishing remain resistant to digital automation.

title: 解体工
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely physical, requiring manual labor and the operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, hazardous environments. While AI might assist in high-level project planning or waste sorting logistics, the core tasks of demolition, scaffolding, and on-site material separation remain resistant to AI automation.

title: 送電線工事
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely physical, involving high-altitude manual labor, equipment rigging, and navigation of unpredictable outdoor environments like mountains and industrial zones. While AI may improve logistics, safety monitoring, or drone-based inspections, the core tasks of stringing and connecting high-voltage lines require human dexterity and physical presence that current AI and robotics cannot replicate in such unstructured settings.

title: 配管工
rationale: Plumbing is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable, non-standardized physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, regulatory compliance checks, or scheduling, the core work of cutting, joining, and installing pipes remains resistant to automation.

title: 鉄筋工
rationale: The core of this job involves physical labor and manual assembly in unpredictable outdoor construction environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI can assist in digital tasks like generating processing lists (加工帳) from blueprints, the physical installation and on-site adjustments remain human-centric despite Japan's push for construction robotics.

title: 鉄道線路管理
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor, manual repairs, and emergency response in unpredictable outdoor environments, which are resistant to AI. While AI and IoT sensors are increasingly used for predictive maintenance and automated track inspection, the physical act of replacing rails, sleepers, and repairing damage still requires human presence and manual skill.

title: 防水工
rationale: Waterproofing is a highly physical trade performed in unpredictable outdoor construction environments that require manual dexterity and site-specific judgment. While AI might assist with administrative scheduling or material estimation, the core task of applying chemical layers and membranes to physical structures remains resistant to automation.

title: 電気工事士
rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical labor in unpredictable construction environments, such as wiring and installing equipment in tight or high spaces. While AI may assist with blueprint analysis or administrative reporting, the physical nature of the work remains highly resistant to automation by current AI and robotics technology.

title: こん包作業員
rationale: While AI and computer vision are improving automated sorting and inspection, the core tasks involve physical manipulation, custom wood-crate construction, and complex spatial packing in unpredictable environments. Japan's labor shortage is driving automation in large logistics centers, but manual dexterity and physical presence remain essential for non-standardized goods and export-grade heavy packaging.

title: ごみ収集作業員
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely physical, requiring manual labor in unpredictable outdoor environments and the handling of diverse physical objects. While AI might optimize collection routes, the core tasks of loading waste and navigating physical obstacles cannot be performed by AI or current robotics technology in a cost-effective way.

title: ハウスクリーニング
rationale: House cleaning is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable, non-standardized environments that are difficult for current robotics to navigate. While AI can optimize scheduling, customer communication, and quoting, the core tasks of manual scrubbing and equipment maintenance remain resistant to automation in the near decade.

title: バックヤード作業員（スーパー食品部門）
rationale: The core tasks involve physical manipulation of food, manual packaging, and shelf stocking in a dynamic retail environment, which are difficult for AI to automate without expensive robotics. While AI will optimize inventory management, pricing (dynamic discounting), and shift scheduling, the physical labor remains essential and is currently more cost-effective than robotic alternatives in Japan's retail sector.

title: ビル清掃
rationale: While autonomous cleaning robots are increasingly common in large Japanese office buildings and malls, the core job still requires significant manual dexterity for tasks like cleaning toilets, wiping windows, and navigating cluttered indoor environments. AI and robotics will likely handle routine floor cleaning, but human workers remain essential for detailed cleaning, waste management, and adapting to unpredictable physical spaces.

title: ピッキング作業員
rationale: While the digital management of inventory is highly automated, the core task remains a physical activity in a complex, 3D environment. Although robotics and AI-driven sorting are advancing, the high cost of full automation and the need for human dexterity in handling diverse product shapes mean humans will remain essential in many Japanese warehouses for the near future.

title: ペストコントロール従事者（害虫等防除・駆除従事者）
rationale: The core of this job involves physical labor in unpredictable environments, such as crawling under floors or into ceilings, which is highly resistant to AI and robotics. While AI can assist with pest identification via image recognition and optimize chemical usage through Integrated Pest Management (IPM) data analysis, the physical application and site-specific problem solving require a human presence. Japan's aging infrastructure and increasing frequency of natural disasters sustain a high demand for this manual, on-site expertise.

title: 倉庫作業員
rationale: While core physical tasks like loading and unpacking require human presence, AI and robotics are increasingly automating inventory management, sorting, and forklift operations. Japan's severe labor shortage in logistics is accelerating the adoption of automated guided vehicles (AGVs) and AI-driven warehouse management systems, though full automation of irregular cargo remains a challenge.

title: 客室清掃・整備担当（ホテル・旅館）
rationale: The core tasks are physical and manual, requiring movement through unpredictable environments and high dexterity for activities like bed-making and cleaning tight spaces. While AI can optimize scheduling and inventory management, the physical labor remains difficult to automate with current robotics, though Japan's labor shortage may drive slow adoption of specialized cleaning robots for floor surfaces.

title: 工場労務作業員
rationale: This occupation consists almost entirely of physical tasks in a manual environment, such as cleaning, waste sorting, and moving physical goods, which are resistant to pure AI software. While robotics and IoT may eventually automate some of these tasks, the current role relies on human physical presence and manual dexterity in varied, non-standardized factory settings where full automation is often cost-prohibitive.

title: 引越作業員
rationale: The core tasks are highly physical, involving the manual handling of heavy, irregularly shaped objects in unpredictable residential environments that are difficult to automate with current robotics. While AI may optimize scheduling and route planning, the fundamental labor remains manual and requires real-time human coordination and physical dexterity.

title: 港湾荷役作業員
rationale: The core of this job involves physical labor and the operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, which are resistant to pure AI replacement. While AI and automation are increasingly used in container terminal logistics and crane optimization, the need for human dexterity in 'slings and hitches' (tamagake) and real-time coordination in diverse port conditions keeps exposure relatively low.

title: 積卸作業員
rationale: While the core tasks are physical and require real-time spatial awareness in unpredictable environments, AI is increasingly optimizing the peripheral tasks of sorting, route planning, and inventory management. Japan's severe labor shortage in logistics is accelerating the adoption of automated guided vehicles (AGVs) and AI-driven warehouse management systems, though manual loading and unloading remain difficult to fully automate.

title: 製品包装作業員
rationale: The core of this job involves physical handling, machine maintenance, and onsite troubleshooting in a factory environment, which limits direct AI replacement. While AI will improve automated inspection (quality control) and predictive maintenance, the physical tasks of replenishing materials and operating forklifts remain resistant to pure AI software, though they are subject to traditional robotics.

title: 鉄道車両清掃
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical and takes place in a complex, mobile environment (train cars and depots) that is difficult for current AI to navigate autonomously. While AI may optimize scheduling and some automated exterior washing already exists, the core tasks of interior manual cleaning, mopping, and seat maintenance remain highly dependent on human labor.

title: ハンバーガーショップ店長
rationale: The role involves a mix of digital management tasks (sales analysis, inventory ordering, and scheduling) which are highly susceptible to AI optimization, and physical/interpersonal tasks (staff training, customer service, and food preparation) that require human presence. While AI can automate the 'desk work' portion of store management, the physical nature of a fast-food environment and the need for on-site leadership in Japan's labor-strapped service sector maintain a need for human oversight.

title: 会社経営者
rationale: AI significantly enhances executive functions like strategic analysis, financial forecasting, and data-driven decision-making, allowing leaders to process vast amounts of information more efficiently. However, the core of the role involves high-stakes accountability, human leadership, and building trust-based relationships, which remain resistant to automation in the Japanese corporate culture.

title: 営業課長
rationale: As a white-collar management role, much of the work involves digital tasks like sales strategy planning, performance analysis, and reporting, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. While human-centric leadership, coaching, and high-stakes client negotiations provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the administrative and analytical burdens of the role, allowing one manager to oversee larger teams or more complex territories.

title: 国会議員
rationale: While the core functions of voting and representation are human-centric and legally protected, AI will heavily reshape the legislative process through automated legal research, policy simulation, and constituent sentiment analysis. Japanese Diet members will increasingly rely on AI to draft bills and manage massive amounts of digital communication, though the final decision-making and interpersonal negotiation remain human-driven.

title: 経理課長
rationale: As a management role focused on digital information processing, this position is highly exposed to AI tools that automate bookkeeping, financial reporting, and compliance checks. While the managerial aspects (leadership, personnel evaluation, and cross-departmental negotiation) provide a buffer, the core technical oversight is being transformed by AI-driven automation of data entry and anomaly detection, shifting the role toward high-level strategic analysis.

title: 総務課長
rationale: The role involves heavy information processing, document management, and legal compliance, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains. While the position requires human-centric leadership and physical coordination (e.g., facility management, crisis response), the core administrative and planning tasks are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools, significantly reducing the human labor required for oversight.

title: 航空機開発エンジニア（ジェットエンジン）
rationale: The core tasks of jet engine engineering—including aerodynamic analysis, structural simulation, and detailed CAD design—are heavily digital and increasingly driven by AI-optimized generative design and predictive modeling. While physical testing and strict regulatory certification (FAA/EASA) provide a buffer requiring human accountability, the vast majority of the work product is digital data and documentation, making the role highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity leaps.

title: 起業、創業
rationale: Entrepreneurs are highly exposed to AI because core tasks like business planning, market analysis, financial modeling, and pitch deck creation are being revolutionized by generative AI tools. While the ultimate human elements of vision, risk-taking, and high-stakes networking remain, AI significantly lowers the barrier to entry and increases the productivity of a small founding team, potentially reducing the number of early-stage employees needed.

title: 銀行支店長
rationale: While branch managers perform high-level relationship management and leadership, their core functions of credit risk assessment, financial analysis, and performance tracking are increasingly automated by AI. Japan's banking sector is aggressively consolidating physical branches and digitizing services, significantly reducing the demand for traditional branch manager roles.

title: 3Dプリンター技術者
rationale: The core of this role involves 3D CAD modeling and digital data preparation, tasks that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and generative design optimization. While physical post-processing and material handling provide a buffer, AI will significantly streamline the 'pre-processing' phase and enhance the ability to predict printing failures, drastically increasing individual productivity.

title: CADオペレーター
rationale: CAD operation is a digital-first knowledge task where AI is rapidly advancing in generative design, automated drafting, and error detection. While high-level engineering judgment remains human, the routine task of converting sketches into technical drawings is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and significant productivity gains, reducing the total number of operators needed.

title: NC工作機械オペレーター
rationale: The core of this role is shifting from manual operation to digital programming (CAD/CAM) and simulation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automated code generation. While physical tasks like material setup and tool maintenance remain, AI is increasingly capable of automating complex path planning and real-time process monitoring, significantly reducing the human labor required per machine.

title: かばん・袋物製造
rationale: The core tasks involve physical manipulation of irregular natural materials (leather) and intricate manual sewing, which are difficult to fully automate in the small-batch, high-variety production environments typical of Japan's bag industry. While AI can optimize design, pattern-making, and material nesting to reduce waste, the physical craftsmanship and sensory judgment required for finishing high-quality goods remain human-centric.

title: かん詰・びん詰・レトルト食品製造
rationale: While the core manufacturing process is heavily mechanized, the role still requires significant physical presence for raw material sorting, manual preparation, and machine operation in a factory environment. AI will likely improve quality control via computer vision and optimize production scheduling, but it cannot yet replace the physical handling of diverse biological raw materials (produce and seafood) that characterize this industry in Japan.

title: しょうゆ製造
rationale: While the physical handling and bottling are already largely mechanized in Japan, AI will significantly impact the complex fermentation monitoring (moromi) by analyzing sensor data to optimize aging. However, the job remains grounded in physical factory environments and requires human sensory judgment for quality control and traditional craftsmanship that resists full automation.

title: みそ製造
rationale: Miso production is a physical manufacturing process involving the handling of raw ingredients and biological fermentation management. While AI can optimize fermentation temperatures and supply chain logistics, the core work remains hands-on and is already heavily mechanized with industrial robotics, leaving little room for significant new AI-driven restructuring.

title: めっき工
rationale: Plating is a physical manufacturing process that has already seen significant automation through specialized machinery and robotics. While AI can optimize chemical compositions and current adjustments, the core job remains tied to physical handling, equipment maintenance, and managing hazardous materials in a high-temperature environment, which limits the impact of generative or digital-first AI.

title: アニメーター
rationale: The work product is entirely digital, and generative AI is already capable of automating labor-intensive tasks like in-betweening (douga), coloring, and background generation. While high-level creative direction and character design still require human judgment, the vast majority of the workforce involved in routine production is highly susceptible to AI-driven restructuring.

title: エレベーター据付
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical and takes place in unpredictable construction environments, requiring manual dexterity and on-site coordination. While AI may assist with scheduling, safety monitoring, or technical diagnostics, the core tasks of physical installation, wiring, and mechanical assembly cannot be automated by current AI or robotics technology.

title: ガラス食器製造
rationale: This occupation involves high-temperature physical labor and artisanal manual dexterity that is difficult for AI to replicate in non-standardized environments. While industrial automation already handles mass production, the core human roles involve physical sensory feedback, craft-based 'shokunin' skills, and equipment maintenance in harsh thermal conditions.

title: タイヤ製造
rationale: Tire manufacturing is a heavily physical and machine-oriented process involving raw material handling and complex chemical processes like vulcanization. While AI can optimize production schedules and predictive maintenance for the machinery, the core work requires physical presence in a factory environment and manual oversight of large-scale industrial equipment.

title: ハム・ソーセージ・ベーコン製造
rationale: The core tasks involve physical handling of meat, machine operation, and sensory quality control in a factory environment, which are resistant to pure AI. While AI can optimize curing schedules and supply chain logistics, the manual labor required for trimming, shaping, and stuffing meat remains high, especially in Japan's specialized food processing sector.

title: パタンナー
rationale: The core of the work is increasingly digital, utilizing CAD software to translate 2D designs into 3D patterns, a process highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While high-end fashion still requires human aesthetic judgment and physical draping (立体裁断), the majority of industrial pattern-making and grading can be significantly streamlined by AI, reducing the total number of workers needed in the Japanese apparel industry.

title: パン製造、パン職人
rationale: While large-scale factories already use automation for mass production, the core of the profession involves physical manipulation of dough and sensory judgment (smell, touch, visual cues) in a physical environment. AI will increasingly optimize fermentation variables and supply chain management, but the manual labor of artisanal shaping and the physical operation of equipment remain resistant to full digital replacement.

title: ビール製造
rationale: The core of the job involves physical labor, equipment sanitation, and manual handling of ingredients, which are resistant to AI. However, AI is increasingly used in large-scale breweries for process optimization and quality control, and in craft brewing for recipe development and flavor profile analysis, augmenting the knowledge-work aspect of the role.

title: プラスチック成形
rationale: While the core physical tasks of material handling and mold installation are already heavily automated by industrial robots, AI will increasingly take over the 'monitoring and adjustment' phase by predicting defects and optimizing molding conditions in real-time. However, the job still requires physical presence for machine maintenance, mold changes, and manual quality inspections, preventing full digital replacement.

title: ミシン縫製
rationale: While the core task involves physical manipulation of soft fabrics which is difficult for AI to handle, the occupation is increasingly impacted by automated sewing systems and computerized pattern integration. In Japan's aging labor market, there is a strong push to digitize the skills of master tailors into automated data, though high-end 'marunui' (complete garment sewing) still requires significant human dexterity and judgment.

title: ワイン製造
rationale: Wine production involves significant physical labor and sensory evaluation (tasting) that remains difficult to digitize fully. While AI can optimize fermentation variables and chemical analysis, the core tasks of physical handling, equipment maintenance, and the cultural value of 'human craftsmanship' in Japanese winemaking provide a buffer against total automation.

title: 光学機器組立
rationale: While AI and computer vision can assist in reading blueprints and quality control, the core of this job involves highly delicate manual assembly of physical components like lenses and shutters. The job description explicitly notes that fully automating these tasks with robots remains difficult, and Japan's aging workforce ensures that human expertise in precision craftsmanship remains critical even as digital tools assist in the process.

title: 化学製品製造オペレーター
rationale: The 'indoor' monitoring tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven predictive maintenance and automated process control, which can significantly optimize chemical reactions and anomaly detection. However, the 'outdoor' component involving physical inspections, manual equipment switching, and complex maintenance during plant shutdowns requires physical presence and dexterity that AI cannot yet replicate.

title: 化粧品製造
rationale: While the manufacturing process is increasingly automated, the role still requires physical presence for machine troubleshooting, manual quality inspections (sight and smell), and handling semi-automated lines. AI will improve quality control via computer vision, but the core physical tasks and equipment maintenance in a factory environment limit total exposure.

title: 医療用画像機器組立
rationale: This occupation involves highly physical and manual tasks such as assembling heavy components with cranes, soldering, and wiring in a physical factory environment. While AI will improve quality control inspections and optimize assembly sequences, the core work requires manual dexterity and physical presence that current AI and robotics cannot fully automate in low-volume, high-complexity medical manufacturing.

title: 医薬品製造
rationale: Pharmaceutical manufacturing in Japan is already highly automated, but AI will significantly enhance the monitoring, quality control, and predictive maintenance tasks currently performed by humans. While physical presence is still required for machine handling and facility management, AI's ability to analyze complex chemical data and ensure regulatory compliance (GMP) will reduce the need for manual oversight.

title: 半導体製造
rationale: The role is already highly automated with workers primarily monitoring equipment and following strict manuals, tasks where AI excels at anomaly detection and process optimization. However, the physical requirement to operate within a cleanroom, handle delicate materials, and perform manual troubleshooting/maintenance provides a significant buffer against full AI replacement in the near term.

title: 印刷オペレーター
rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including machine maintenance, material handling, and hardware troubleshooting, which limits full automation. However, AI is rapidly advancing in automated color management, predictive maintenance, and print job optimization, reducing the level of human expertise required for quality control and technical adjustments.

title: 合板製造
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical and takes place in a factory environment involving heavy machinery and manual handling of wood materials. While AI can optimize production parameters (cutting conditions, drying times) and improve quality inspection via computer vision, the core tasks require physical presence and manual labor that are more susceptible to traditional industrial robotics than generative AI.

title: 和菓子製造、和菓子職人
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical dexterity, sensory evaluation (taste and smell), and artistic craftsmanship in a non-digital environment. While AI can optimize recipes, inventory, and large-scale factory production, the traditional Japanese Wagashi artisan relies on manual skills and cultural aesthetics that are difficult to automate in small-scale, high-quality shops.

title: 太陽光発電のメンテナンス
rationale: The core of the job involves physical inspection, manual repairs, and site management (like weeding and cleaning) in unpredictable outdoor environments, which are resistant to AI. While AI and IoT enhance remote monitoring and predictive diagnostics, the 'last mile' of troubleshooting and physical maintenance still requires human technicians with specialized electrical certifications.

title: 家具製造
rationale: Furniture manufacturing is primarily a physical occupation requiring manual dexterity and the handling of physical materials in a workshop environment. While AI and digital automation have already streamlined mass production and design phases, the core tasks of assembly, finishing, and custom craftsmanship remain resistant to full AI replacement due to the tactile nature of wood and the need for human sensory judgment.

title: 家電修理
rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, manual tool usage, and navigating unpredictable home environments, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While AI will significantly improve diagnostic accuracy and troubleshooting through advanced software and sensors, the physical act of replacing components and performing on-site repairs remains a human-centric task.

title: 建具製造
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical, requiring manual dexterity and on-site installation in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may optimize supply chains or design patterns, the core tasks of cutting, assembling, and fitting wooden fixtures (especially traditional Japanese items like fusuma and shoji) remain resistant to digital automation.

title: 建築塗装工
rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical labor in unpredictable outdoor and indoor environments, which are highly resistant to AI and robotics. While AI may assist in peripheral tasks like color simulation, material diagnostics, or project scheduling, the physical application of paint and surface preparation remains a human-centric task in the Japanese construction industry.

title: 建築板金
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely physical, involving manual labor on roofs and construction sites with unpredictable environments and safety risks. While AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like cost estimation or design, the core work of cutting, bending, and installing metal sheets remains a hands-on human task that is highly resistant to automation.

title: 惣菜製造
rationale: While the industry is increasingly mechanized, the core work remains physical and takes place in a high-hygiene, real-time environment involving manual dexterity for delicate food handling. AI's impact is limited to peripheral tasks like demand forecasting, inventory management, and automated quality inspection, but it cannot yet replace the physical labor of diverse food preparation and packing.

title: 木材製造
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical and takes place in a high-risk industrial environment involving heavy machinery and raw materials. While AI can optimize timber yield calculations and automate some sorting (grading) tasks, the core work requires physical presence, manual handling, and the operation of specialized hardware that is already partially automated by traditional robotics rather than generative AI.

title: 染色工/染色設備オペレーター
rationale: While the core physical process is increasingly automated and digitally controlled, the role requires a mix of chemical knowledge, sensory judgment (touch and color), and physical oversight of machinery. AI will significantly optimize chemical mixing and quality inspection, but the transition toward high-end, small-batch production in Japan preserves the need for human aesthetic sensibility and physical handling.

title: 検査工（工業製品）
rationale: Visual inspection and data-driven quality control are primary targets for computer vision and machine learning, which can identify defects more consistently than humans. While some physical handling and complex sensory tasks (like tactile checks) remain, the shift toward automated in-line inspection and AI-powered image processing is rapidly reducing the need for manual inspectors in Japanese manufacturing.

title: 検査工（食料品等）
rationale: While the job involves physical sampling and lab work, the core tasks of visual inspection (detecting defects/foreign objects) and data analysis are highly susceptible to AI-driven computer vision and automated sensor integration. Japan's labor shortage is actively driving the replacement of human visual inspectors with AI systems, though high-level sensory evaluation (taste/smell) and complex lab troubleshooting provide a partial buffer.

title: 水産ねり製品製造
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical and takes place in a factory environment, which provides a natural buffer against AI. While AI can optimize recipes (raikai process) and monitor quality control via computer vision, the core tasks involve physical handling, machine operation, and maintenance in a wet, variable environment that is already heavily automated by traditional robotics rather than AI.

title: 汎用金属工作機械工（旋盤工、ボール盤工等）
rationale: This occupation relies heavily on physical manual dexterity, sensory feedback, and 'craftsman' intuition for small-batch or prototype production that is difficult to automate fully. While AI and advanced NC machines handle mass production, the core of this role involves physical manipulation of machinery and real-time adjustments in a non-digital environment, though AI may increasingly assist in the initial 'simulation' and planning phases.

title: 清酒製造
rationale: Sake brewing involves a significant physical component and sensory expertise (smell, taste, touch) that remains difficult for AI to fully replicate. However, AI and IoT sensors are increasingly used to monitor fermentation and automate temperature controls, augmenting the traditional 'intuition' of the Toji (master brewer) and reducing the need for manual labor in large-scale production.

title: 溶接工
rationale: Welding is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity and real-time adjustments in unpredictable environments like construction sites and shipyards. While industrial robots and AI-driven automated welding are common in controlled factory settings, a significant portion of the work remains manual or semi-automatic, requiring human presence and physical labor that AI cannot currently replicate.

title: 漆器製造
rationale: Lacquerware production is a highly physical, manual craft requiring delicate hand-eye coordination and sensory judgment that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist in peripheral areas like business management or generative design for patterns, the core tasks of carving, layering lacquer, and intricate decoration remain deeply rooted in physical craftsmanship and traditional techniques.

title: 物流設備管理・保全
rationale: While AI significantly enhances predictive maintenance (予知保全) and data analysis for equipment health, the core job requires physical presence to perform manual repairs, sensory inspections (sight/sound), and hardware installation. Japan's labor shortage accelerates the use of AI for monitoring, but the tactile complexity of fixing diverse mechanical failures in a physical warehouse remains a barrier to full automation.

title: 玩具（おもちゃ）製作
rationale: The core creative tasks of toy manufacturing—ideation, design, and 3D modeling—are highly susceptible to Generative AI and AI-assisted CAD tools, which significantly accelerate the development cycle. While physical production and quality control involve manual labor or machine operation, the shift toward digital prototyping and the high reliance on trend analysis and digital design place this occupation in a high-exposure category. Japan's labor shortage further incentivizes the automation of the production management and inspection tasks described.

title: 生産用機械組立
rationale: This occupation involves significant physical labor, spatial reasoning, and manual dexterity in unpredictable environments, especially for custom-made (cell production) machinery which is difficult to automate. While AI will enhance peripheral tasks like design-to-assembly instructions, quality inspections, and predictive maintenance, the core physical assembly and on-site installation remain reliant on human skill and physical presence.

title: 産業用ロボットの保守・メンテナンス
rationale: While AI and IoT sensors significantly enhance predictive maintenance and diagnostic capabilities, the core of the job remains highly physical, requiring manual dexterity to replace parts, clean components, and perform on-site repairs in diverse factory environments. Japan's labor shortage in technical maintenance roles ensures that AI will act as a supportive tool for efficiency rather than a replacement for the essential human presence required for safety-regulated physical inspections.

title: 看板制作
rationale: The design and planning phases are highly exposed to AI tools for graphic generation and regulatory compliance checks. However, the core of the job involves physical manufacturing, transportation, and onsite installation using cranes and manual labor, which remain resistant to automation.

title: 石工
rationale: The core of a stonemason's work involves heavy physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time adaptation to the unique physical properties of natural stone in outdoor or construction environments. While AI and computer-controlled machinery (CNC) assist in the initial cutting and design phases, the physical installation, site-specific 'ishi-zumi' (stone stacking), and intricate hand-finishing remain highly resistant to AI automation.

title: 石油精製オペレーター
rationale: The role is already highly digitized, with 'boardmen' monitoring processes via computer screens and 'fieldmen' following automated protocols. AI is increasingly capable of optimizing complex chemical reactions and predictive maintenance, significantly reducing the need for human monitoring and manual intervention in these highly controlled environments.

title: 紙器製造
rationale: While the manufacturing process is increasingly mechanized, the core work remains physical and involves handling tangible materials in a factory environment. AI's impact is limited to optimizing machine settings and production scheduling, but it cannot replace the manual dexterity required for high-end handmade boxes or the physical oversight of heavy machinery.

title: 紡績機械オペレーター
rationale: While the industry has already seen significant automation and computer-integrated manufacturing, the core role still requires physical presence to handle manual tasks like splicing broken threads and feeding raw materials. AI will likely optimize production schedules and predictive maintenance, but it cannot yet replace the tactile dexterity and real-time physical troubleshooting required on the factory floor.

title: 紡織設備管理・保全
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical, requiring manual dexterity and on-site presence to repair, assemble, and maintain heavy textile machinery. While AI and IoT can enhance predictive maintenance and parts management (shifting some cognitive tasks), the core work of physical troubleshooting and mechanical adjustment in a complex factory environment remains difficult to automate fully.

title: 織布工/織機オペレーター
rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of machinery, manual threading, and tactile quality inspections that are difficult to fully automate with AI alone. While computer-controlled looms and automated monitoring systems exist, the human operator's role in Japan is increasingly focused on high-end, complex textiles where human sensory observation and manual dexterity are required to handle delicate threads and maintain quality.

title: 自動車整備士
rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation, repair, and inspection of hardware in a non-standardized environment, which remains difficult for AI to automate. While AI and diagnostic 'scan tools' significantly assist in fault detection and electronic systems analysis, the manual labor of replacing parts and physical maintenance ensures the role remains largely human-centric. Japan's labor shortage in this sector is driving the adoption of AI-assisted diagnostics, but the physical nature of the work provides a strong buffer against full automation.

title: 自動車板金塗装
rationale: The core tasks of panel beating and painting are highly physical, requiring manual dexterity and sensory judgment in unpredictable, non-standardized repair scenarios. While AI can assist in peripheral areas like automated damage estimation (via computer vision) and color matching databases, the physical execution remains resistant to automation, though advanced safety sensors in modern cars may eventually reduce overall market demand for collision repairs.

title: 自動車検査員
rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including manual inspections with hammers and visual checks in a workshop environment, which limits full automation. However, the introduction of OBD (On-Board Diagnostics) electronic testing and AI-driven defect detection systems are already digitizing the 'judgment' aspect of the job, shifting the worker's role toward supervising automated diagnostic outputs.

title: 自動車組立
rationale: Automobile assembly in Japan is already highly automated with industrial robots, leaving humans to handle complex tactile tasks, final inspections, and supervisory roles that are difficult to digitize. While AI will improve logistics and error detection (POKA-YOKE), the core of the job remains a physical, on-site activity in a high-stakes manufacturing environment.

title: 航空整備士
rationale: While AI and predictive maintenance sensors significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy and parts management, the core job remains highly physical, requiring manual dexterity and sensory inspection in complex environments. Legal accountability and the requirement for a certified human 'confirming mechanic' to sign off on flight safety provide a strong regulatory barrier against full automation.

title: 花火師
rationale: The core of the job involves manual manufacturing of explosives and heavy physical labor at event sites, which are resistant to AI due to safety regulations and unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI can assist in digital choreography and show design, the physical handling of hazardous materials and the 'human touch' required for traditional craftsmanship remain central.

title: 製版オペレーター、DTPオペレーター
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the manipulation of text and images through software like Photoshop and Illustrator, which are domains where AI is already automating layout, color correction, and image generation. While some human oversight remains for final quality control and client communication, the core technical tasks of DTP and prepress are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.

title: 計器組立
rationale: This occupation involves high-precision physical assembly and manual adjustment of diverse, small-batch instruments that are difficult to fully automate with current robotics. While AI can optimize production scheduling and quality inspection (computer vision), the core tasks require manual dexterity, physical tools, and human tactile feedback in a non-standardized environment.

title: 豆腐製造、豆腐職人
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and sensory judgment in a wet, food-production environment. While AI can optimize supply chains or monitor fermentation/temperature data, the physical tasks of handling soy products, deep-frying, and equipment maintenance remain resistant to AI, especially in traditional small-scale shops (machi-tofu) common in Japan.

title: 貴金属装身具製作
rationale: While AI and 3D printing (CAD/CAM) are significantly transforming the design and prototyping phases of jewelry making, the core of the work remains a physical craft requiring manual dexterity, soldering, and stone-setting. In Japan, the high value of materials and the emphasis on 'craftsmanship' (monozukuri) provide a buffer against full automation, though AI will increasingly assist in creative design and custom pattern generation.

title: 造船技能者（造船工、船舶艤装工等）
rationale: Shipbuilding is a heavy industrial occupation requiring physical labor, manual dexterity, and presence in complex, unpredictable environments like docks and ship hulls. While AI and CAD/CAM have automated design, marking, and some robotic welding, the core tasks of 'gyotsu' (manual steel bending), complex assembly in tight spaces, and final outfitting remain highly dependent on human skill and physical presence.

title: 配電盤・制御盤等組立
rationale: The core of this job involves manual assembly, wiring, and physical testing of electrical panels in a non-standardized environment, which is difficult for current AI or robotics to automate fully. AI's impact is limited to peripheral tasks like digitalizing blueprints (DX), optimizing wiring sequences, and assisting in quality control checks via computer vision. While productivity may increase through better information management, the physical dexterity and human double-checking required for safety-critical electrical infrastructure remain essential.

title: 野菜つけ物製造
rationale: The core tasks involve physical handling of organic materials, such as sorting, washing, and cutting vegetables, which are difficult to fully automate due to natural variations in shape and quality. While AI can optimize supply chains and quality control sensors, the physical nature of the work and the prevalence of small-scale regional producers in Japan limit the scope for total AI replacement.

title: 金型工
rationale: The role is a hybrid of digital design (CAD/CAM) and physical craftsmanship. While AI and advanced software are significantly automating the design and precision machining phases, the final assembly, sub-micron manual polishing, and physical troubleshooting of custom molds still require high-level human tactile skill and situational judgment that are difficult to fully automate.

title: 金属プレス工
rationale: While automation and sensors are highly integrated into Japanese metal pressing, the core role still requires physical presence for mold installation, manual adjustments, and equipment maintenance. AI will improve monitoring and quality control, but the physical nature of handling heavy machinery and materials in a factory environment limits total digital displacement.

title: 鉄鋼製造オペレーター
rationale: Steel manufacturing is already highly automated, but AI is increasingly taking over the complex monitoring and predictive maintenance tasks previously handled by experienced human operators. While physical presence and manual intervention for troubleshooting remain necessary, AI's ability to optimize furnace temperatures and rolling processes directly impacts the core decision-making and 'craftsmanship' elements of the role.

title: 鉄骨工
rationale: While AI and CAD/CAM systems are increasingly used for drafting and automated material processing, the core of the job involves heavy physical labor, manual welding, and complex assembly in a factory environment. The reliance on human 'experience and intuition' for assembly and the non-routine nature of custom-built steel structures provide significant protection against full AI automation.

title: 鋳造工/鋳造設備オペレーター
rationale: The core of the job involves physical handling of molten metal and molds in a high-heat environment, which remains difficult to fully automate with AI alone.
