While AI is increasingly used for predictive maintenance of furnaces and optimizing casting parameters to reduce defects, the physical presence and manual intervention of skilled operators are still essential in Japan's many specialized SME foundries.

title: 録音エンジニア
rationale: The core tasks of mixing, mastering, and noise reduction are increasingly automated by AI tools that can balance tracks and match professional sonic profiles instantly. While physical microphone setup and interpersonal communication with artists remain human-centric, the digital nature of the final output makes the profession highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and displacement of entry-level roles.

title: 鍛造工/鍛造設備オペレーター
rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of heavy materials and real-time sensory judgment in a high-heat, high-pressure environment. While AI and robotics are increasingly automating routine press operations and monitoring, the high-skill 'free forging' and complex mold adjustments still require human dexterity and years of physical experience that are difficult to digitize.

title: 陶磁器製造
rationale: While industrial ceramics manufacturing already utilizes automation and robots, the core of the work remains physical and involves handling raw materials in a tactile environment. AI will likely optimize kiln temperatures and quality inspection through computer vision, but the manual dexterity required for molding, glazing, and traditional artistic painting provides a significant buffer against full automation.

title: 電子機器組立
rationale: While much of the physical assembly is already automated or handled by manual labor, AI will significantly impact the monitoring, quality control, and adjustment tasks described in the job profile. AI-driven computer vision and predictive maintenance will increasingly replace human inspection and manual machine calibration, though the physical dexterity required for non-automated lines remains a barrier.

title: 非破壊検査技術者
rationale: The core of the job is shifting from manual inspection to AI-driven image and sensor data analysis, where machine learning models can identify defects in X-rays or ultrasonic data more accurately than humans. However, the physical requirement to access infrastructure sites (bridges, plants, tunnels) and the legal responsibility for safety certifications provide a significant buffer against full automation in the Japanese regulatory environment.

title: 靴製造
rationale: The design and pattern-making phases are highly exposed to AI and CAD integration, but the core manufacturing involves complex physical manipulation of tactile materials like leather. While automation exists for mass production, the industry's shift toward high-value, small-batch, and bespoke products preserves the need for human sensory judgment and manual dexterity.

title: 風力発電のメンテナンス
rationale: The core of the job involves high-altitude physical labor, manual repairs of composite materials, and complex mechanical troubleshooting in unpredictable outdoor environments, which remain difficult for AI and robotics to replicate. While AI and drones are increasingly used for remote monitoring and visual inspections, the physical act of climbing, grinding, and repairing blades requires human dexterity and real-time physical judgment. Japan's labor shortage in this sector is more likely to lead to AI-assisted tools (like diagnostic drones) rather than full automation of the maintenance technician.

title: AIエンジニア
rationale: AI Engineers are at the epicenter of the technology, meaning their core tasks—coding, data analysis, and model architecture—are highly susceptible to being automated by the very tools they create (e.g., GitHub Copilot, AutoML). While they remain essential for high-level strategy and oversight, the productivity gains from AI mean the nature of the role is shifting from manual coding to high-level system orchestration and prompt engineering.

title: CG制作
rationale: CG production is a purely digital field where Generative AI is already capable of automating core tasks like texturing, background generation, and even complex 3D modeling and animation. While high-level creative direction and specialized quality control remain human-led, the productivity gains from AI mean a significant restructuring of the workforce and the displacement of entry-level roles.

title: DXプロデューサー
rationale: As a role centered on digital transformation, DX Producers heavily utilize AI for data analysis, solution architecting, and content generation, significantly increasing their individual productivity. While the core tasks of high-level stakeholder negotiation, ethical judgment, and creative visioning remain human-centric, the technical and analytical components of the job are highly exposed to AI integration.

title: ITコンサルタント
rationale: IT consulting is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—data analysis, strategy formulation, and technical documentation—are being radically accelerated by generative AI. While the role requires human-centric skills like stakeholder management and high-level judgment, the 'digital' nature of the work product and the shift toward AI-driven DX solutions mean the profession will undergo significant restructuring and productivity gains.

title: M&Aマネージャー、M&Aコンサルタント/M&Aアドバイザー
rationale: M&A work is highly digital and information-intensive, involving extensive data analysis, due diligence, and document preparation (LOIs, contracts, valuation reports) where AI can provide massive productivity gains. While the core of the job requires high-stakes human negotiation, emotional intelligence, and complex judgment, AI will increasingly automate the technical 'heavy lifting' of financial modeling and legal review, significantly reducing the number of man-hours required per deal.

title: UX/UIデザイナー
rationale: UX/UI design is a digital-first occupation where AI tools can now generate wireframes, layouts, and even functional code from text prompts or sketches. While high-level user research and stakeholder management require human empathy and negotiation, the core production tasks—visual design, prototyping, and usability testing—are being rapidly automated or augmented by AI, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for large design teams.

title: Webディレクター（Web制作会社）
rationale: The core output of this role is digital, and AI is rapidly automating the primary tasks overseen by Web Directors, including coding, UI/UX design, and content generation. While human-centric skills like client negotiation and project management remain necessary, AI-driven productivity gains will significantly reduce the number of directors needed to manage a project and lower the barrier for clients to bypass agencies entirely.

title: Webデザイナー（Web制作会社）
rationale: Web design is a fundamentally digital occupation where core tasks—layout creation, image generation, and coding (HTML/CSS)—are being rapidly automated by Generative AI and no-code tools. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, the technical production work that defines the majority of the role in Japanese production companies is facing extreme restructuring and productivity shifts.

title: あんまマッサージ指圧師
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and requires real-time human-to-human interaction and manual dexterity to apply pressure to specific points on the body. While AI might assist with administrative tasks or diagnostic suggestions, the core service—physical massage and shiatsu—cannot be replaced by digital AI, and Japan's regulatory framework for licensed medical practitioners provides a significant barrier to automation.

title: はり師・きゅう師
rationale: The core of this occupation is a highly tactile, physical procedure involving the precise insertion of needles and application of heat to the human body, which requires real-time physical feedback and human trust. While AI may assist in diagnostic support, patient record management, or optimizing treatment plans based on Oriental medicine data, the manual execution and interpersonal rapport remain essential and difficult to automate.

title: アウトドアインストラクター
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical activity in unpredictable natural environments and real-time human interaction, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI can assist with administrative tasks, route planning, or environmental data analysis, the primary value lies in physical safety management, hands-on instruction, and human hospitality.

title: アナウンサー
rationale: AI voice synthesis and digital avatars can already perform routine news reading and narration with high accuracy, significantly impacting entry-level tasks. While high-stakes live sports commentary and complex interpersonal interviewing remain difficult to automate, the core information-delivery aspect of the job is highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and substitution.

title: アートディレクター
rationale: The core work product is digital, and generative AI is revolutionizing visual asset creation, layout design, and concept ideation. While the role still requires high-level human judgment for client management and creative direction, AI significantly automates the production tasks of the designers and illustrators they manage, leading to massive productivity gains and team restructuring.

title: イラストレーター
rationale: The work product is entirely digital and generative AI has already demonstrated a high capability to produce complex illustrations, characters, and backgrounds based on text prompts. While high-level creative direction and specific client requirements still require human intervention, the core task of rendering images is being rapidly automated, significantly reducing the time and labor required per piece.

title: インダストリアルデザイナー
rationale: Industrial design is a digital-heavy knowledge profession where AI can now rapidly generate 2D sketches, 3D models, and color variations based on specific parameters. While human judgment is still required for physical ergonomics, manufacturing feasibility, and cross-departmental coordination, AI significantly reduces the time needed for the core creative and drafting phases.

title: インテリアコーディネーター
rationale: The core tasks of visual design, 3D modeling, and material selection are highly susceptible to Generative AI, which can instantly produce high-quality interior renderings and layouts based on client preferences. While the role still requires physical site visits and human-to-human consulting to navigate complex client emotions and Japanese construction regulations, AI will significantly automate the technical production and visualization aspects of the job.

title: インテリアデザイナー
rationale: The core tasks of interior design—generating layouts, selecting color palettes, and creating 3D visualizations—are increasingly being automated by generative AI and advanced CAD tools. While the job requires physical site visits and human-centric empathy for client needs, the shift toward digital-first design processes in Japan's corporate and residential sectors makes this occupation highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task restructuring.

title: エコノミスト
rationale: Economists perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, trend forecasting, and report writing—all areas where LLMs and advanced AI excel. While high-level strategic judgment and public communication remain human-centric, AI's ability to process vast datasets and generate initial drafts of economic reports significantly increases productivity, likely leading to a consolidation of roles within Japanese think tanks and financial institutions.

title: オーケストラ奏者（団員）
rationale: The core of the job is a live, physical performance in a specific acoustic environment, which AI cannot replicate in a concert hall setting. While AI can assist in music composition, score study, or practice tools, the cultural value of an orchestra lies in the real-time human synchronization and emotional expression that audiences pay to experience in person.

title: カウンセラー（医療福祉分野）
rationale: AI is highly capable of analyzing sentiment, providing empathetic text-based responses, and handling administrative tasks like session notes and scheduling. However, the core of clinical counseling in Japan relies on 'rapport' and human-to-human trust, especially in sensitive medical and welfare contexts like child protection where physical presence and non-verbal cues are critical.

title: カラーコーディネーター
rationale: The core tasks of color coordination—analyzing trends, mapping color palettes to brand concepts, and generating visual simulations—are highly digital and align perfectly with Generative AI capabilities. While high-end architectural or personal consulting still requires human empathy and physical site visits, the majority of commercial color design and marketing analysis can now be automated or significantly accelerated by AI tools.

title: グラフィックデザイナー
rationale: Graphic design is a digital-first occupation where Generative AI can now perform core tasks like image creation, layout generation, and color theory application with high speed and quality. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, the technical production work that defines the entry-level and mid-level roles in Japan's design industry is facing massive restructuring and productivity shifts.

title: ゲームクリエーター
rationale: The core tasks of game creation—coding, graphic design, sound production, and scenario writing—are all digital-first domains where Generative AI is already demonstrating transformative capabilities. While high-level creative direction and project management remain human-led, AI tools significantly automate asset generation and routine programming, leading to massive productivity shifts and structural changes in how development teams are staffed.

title: コピーライター
rationale: Copywriting is a purely digital information-processing task where AI models already excel at generating creative catchphrases, structured text, and marketing angles based on data. While high-level creative direction and final client management still require human judgment, the core production of drafts and the synthesis of consumer trends are being rapidly automated by generative AI.

title: システムエンジニア（Webサービス開発）
rationale: Web service development is almost entirely digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and documentation already being revolutionized by LLMs and AI coding assistants. While high-level requirements definition and team collaboration remain human-centric, AI significantly reduces the time required for technical execution, leading to major productivity gains and structural shifts in how these roles are staffed in Japan's tech sector.

title: システムエンジニア（受託開発）
rationale: The core work products—code, design documents, and test plans—are entirely digital and highly susceptible to AI generation and automation. While high-level requirements gathering and client communication provide a buffer, AI significantly accelerates the technical phases (detailed design, coding, and testing), leading to massive productivity gains and a restructuring of the traditional SE-to-programmer hierarchy in Japan.

title: システムエンジニア（基盤システム）
rationale: Infrastructure engineering is increasingly shifting from physical hardware manipulation to 'Infrastructure as Code' (IaC) and cloud-based configurations, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While high-level architecture design and client requirement definition still require human judgment, AI can now generate complex configuration scripts, troubleshoot network issues, and optimize system performance, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for routine manual setup.

title: システムエンジニア（組込み、IoT）
rationale: While core tasks like coding, debugging, and system design are highly susceptible to AI automation, this role requires significant integration with physical hardware and sensors. The need to troubleshoot real-world physical constraints, manage hardware-software interdependencies, and conduct on-site testing in unpredictable environments (like factories or farms) provides a buffer against total AI replacement compared to pure software engineering.

title: ジュエリーデザイナー
rationale: Jewelry design is increasingly digital, relying heavily on CAD software and visual conceptualization, areas where generative AI can now produce high-quality 3D models and intricate patterns from text prompts. While high-end bespoke work and physical craftsmanship remain, the core tasks of trend analysis, sketching, and technical drafting are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.

title: スクールカウンセラー
rationale: While AI can assist with documentation, preliminary psychological screening, and providing resource materials, the core of the role involves high-stakes interpersonal trust, physical presence in schools, and complex non-verbal communication with minors. Japan's regulatory requirements for certified professionals (Public Psychologists/Clinical Psychologists) and the cultural emphasis on face-to-face 'omotenashi' in counseling create significant barriers to full automation.

title: スタイリスト
rationale: While AI can assist with trend analysis, mood board creation, and virtual outfit simulations, the core of the job involves significant physical labor, such as transporting heavy garments, on-site tailoring, and real-time adjustments during shoots. Furthermore, the role requires high-level interpersonal negotiation with apparel brands and creative collaboration with directors, which are difficult to automate in the physical production environments of Japan's media industry.

title: スポーツインストラクター
rationale: While AI and computer vision can provide real-time form correction and personalized workout plans, the core of the job requires physical presence, hands-on safety spotting, and high-touch interpersonal motivation. In Japan's aging society, the role also involves significant human-to-human care and social interaction that technology cannot yet fully replicate.

title: セキュリティエキスパート（オペレーション）
rationale: This role is fundamentally digital, involving the continuous monitoring and analysis of system logs and network traffic, which are tasks highly suited for AI and machine learning. While human judgment remains critical for high-stakes incident response and client communication, AI is already being integrated into SOCs to automate threat detection and initial log analysis, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the headcount needed for routine monitoring.

title: セキュリティエキスパート（デジタルフォレンジック）
rationale: The core tasks of digital forensics—data extraction, log analysis, pattern recognition, and malware investigation—are highly digital and increasingly automated by AI-driven security tools. While human experts are still required for legal testimony, chain-of-custody physical handling, and high-level ethical judgment, AI significantly accelerates the processing of massive datasets and the identification of anomalies that previously required manual inspection.

title: セキュリティエキスパート（情報セキュリティ監査）
rationale: Information security auditing is a digital-centric knowledge profession where AI can automate the analysis of vast documentation, log files, and compliance mapping against complex standards. While high-level human judgment, ethical accountability, and stakeholder interviews remain essential, AI tools will significantly reduce the time required for routine evidence collection and vulnerability assessment, leading to major productivity shifts.

title: セキュリティエキスパート（脆弱性診断）
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital and involves analyzing code, network protocols, and system architectures—tasks where LLMs and specialized AI security tools excel. While 'manual diagnosis' currently requires human intuition, AI is rapidly automating vulnerability discovery, pattern matching in code, and even generating remediation reports, significantly increasing the productivity and reducing the headcount needed for routine assessments.

title: ソフトウェア開発（スマホアプリ）
rationale: Smartphone app development is a purely digital occupation where core tasks—coding, UI design, debugging, and documentation—are being aggressively automated or enhanced by LLMs and generative AI. While high-level product strategy and complex human-centric UX design still require human judgment, AI significantly reduces the headcount needed for implementation, and the rise of no-code/low-code tools further lowers the barrier to entry.

title: ソフトウェア開発（パッケージソフト）
rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—coding, debugging, and software design—are fundamentally digital and are currently being revolutionized by LLM-based coding assistants. While high-level planning and user-centric design still require human judgment, AI can now automate significant portions of the routine programming and testing phases, drastically increasing individual productivity and reducing the total headcount needed for traditional package software development.

title: テキスタイルデザイナー
rationale: Textile design is a digital-heavy creative field where AI can now rapidly generate patterns, simulate fabric textures, and analyze fashion trends. While the final physical production and material selection require human oversight, the core tasks of ideation, color coordination, and pattern drafting are highly susceptible to generative AI automation.

title: テクニカルイラストレーター
rationale: The work is fundamentally digital, relying on CAD and design software to transform technical data into visual illustrations. Generative AI and automated 3D-to-2D conversion tools are rapidly advancing, allowing AI to interpret blueprints and generate precise technical drawings, significantly reducing the manual labor required for drafting and perspective rendering.

title: テクニカルライター
rationale: The core tasks of technical writing—synthesizing technical specifications into clear, structured documentation—are highly digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While human oversight is still needed for physical product testing and interviewing engineers, AI can now automate the vast majority of drafting, formatting, and multi-language translation, significantly reducing the total human labor required per manual.

title: テレビカメラマン
rationale: While the physical act of operating heavy cameras and navigating unpredictable field environments provides a buffer, AI is rapidly automating framing, tracking, and multi-camera switching. In Japan's aging labor market, the shift toward automated studios and AI-driven remote production is accelerating to reduce the need for large human crews.

title: テレビ・ラジオ放送技術者
rationale: While physical tasks like camera operation and equipment maintenance remain, the core functions of switching, audio mixing, and signal transmission are increasingly automated through AI-driven software. Japan's broadcasting industry is rapidly adopting AI for automated subtitling, real-time video processing, and predictive maintenance of transmission infrastructure to address labor shortages and operational efficiency.

title: ディスプレイデザイナー
rationale: The core planning and creative phases—including conceptualization, rough sketching, and 3D modeling—are highly susceptible to generative AI tools that can rapidly produce visual layouts and marketing strategies. However, the role retains a significant physical component involving on-site construction supervision, material selection, and manual adjustments in physical retail environments that AI cannot yet replicate.

title: デバッグ作業
rationale: Debugging is a purely digital information-processing task that is being revolutionized by AI's ability to analyze code, generate test cases, and suggest fixes. While the description emphasizes manual 'brute force' testing by humans, LLMs and automated testing tools are rapidly replacing these routine verification tasks, leaving only the most complex edge cases for human oversight.

title: データエンジニア
rationale: Data engineering is a purely digital occupation where core tasks like writing ETL pipelines, cleaning data, and managing cloud infrastructure are being aggressively automated by Generative AI and specialized AI tools. While human oversight is needed for complex architecture and business alignment, AI significantly reduces the manual labor required for coding and data preprocessing, leading to massive productivity gains and role restructuring.

title: データサイエンティスト
rationale: Data science is fundamentally digital and revolves around coding, statistical modeling, and data analysis—all areas where LLMs and automated machine learning (AutoML) are exceptionally proficient. While human judgment is still needed for business alignment and problem definition, AI can now automate the majority of data cleaning, visualization, and model generation tasks, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the number of workers required for routine analysis.

title: ナレーター
rationale: The core output of this occupation is digital audio, a domain where AI text-to-speech (TTS) and voice cloning technologies have reached near-human quality. While high-end artistic narration still requires human emotion, routine tasks like facility announcements, instructional videos, and news reading are being rapidly replaced by AI in Japan to reduce costs and address labor shortages.

title: ハローワーク職員
rationale: The core tasks of matching job seekers to vacancies, processing insurance claims, and analyzing labor market data are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While high-touch career counseling and support for vulnerable populations require human empathy, the vast majority of the administrative and information-processing workload is digital and ripe for restructuring.

title: バイオテクノロジー技術者
rationale: Biotechnology is heavily impacted by AI through bioinformatics, protein folding predictions, and automated drug discovery, which significantly accelerates the R&D cycle. While physical lab work and equipment maintenance provide a buffer, the core tasks of data analysis, experimental design, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.

title: バイオテクノロジー研究者
rationale: Biotechnology research is heavily driven by data analysis, genomic sequencing, and literature review, all of which are being revolutionized by AI and AlphaFold-like technologies. While physical lab work and complex ethical judgment remain, AI is significantly automating the hypothesis generation, drug discovery, and patent drafting phases of the job.

title: バイオマス発電プラントの設計
rationale: The core tasks of plant design—including CAD modeling, thermal/fluid simulations, and detailed engineering calculations—are highly digital and increasingly enhanced by AI-driven generative design and optimization tools. While the role requires physical site visits and complex stakeholder coordination which provide some protection, the shift toward automated engineering workflows and AI-assisted regulatory compliance significantly increases productivity and reshapes the design process.

title: ピアノ調律師
rationale: Piano tuning is a highly specialized physical craft requiring manual dexterity and real-time sensory feedback in diverse physical environments. While AI and digital tools can assist with pitch detection (tuning), the core tasks of 'seicho' (regulation) and 'seion' (voicing) involve complex mechanical adjustments and subjective artistic collaboration with pianists that remain difficult to automate.

title: ファイナンシャル・プランナー
rationale: The core tasks of data collection, financial analysis, and plan generation are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime candidates for AI automation. While the interpersonal 'consulting' aspect remains important in Japan's aging society, AI can now handle complex tax, pension, and investment simulations more efficiently than humans, shifting the role toward high-level oversight and emotional support.

title: ファインセラミックス製造技術者
rationale: This role combines physical manufacturing oversight with high-level R&D and materials science. AI will significantly accelerate the knowledge-work components, such as materials informatics for discovering new ceramic compositions and optimizing firing conditions, but the physical nature of managing factory equipment and handling specialized hardware provides a buffer against full automation.

title: ファッションデザイナー
rationale: Fashion design is increasingly digital, with AI now capable of generating high-fidelity sketches, predicting trends from vast datasets, and automating pattern-making. While the physical aspects of fabric selection and final tailoring remain, the core creative and analytical tasks—market analysis, conceptualization, and visual design—are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.

title: ファンドマネージャー
rationale: The core tasks of data analysis, economic forecasting, and portfolio optimization are highly susceptible to AI and machine learning, which can process vast datasets faster than humans. While senior managers still provide critical human judgment and client relationship management, AI is significantly automating the research and execution functions, leading to higher productivity and potential workforce consolidation.

title: フラワーデザイナー
rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, handling delicate organic materials, and manual labor like transporting heavy buckets of water, which are difficult to automate. While AI can assist in the creative phase by generating design concepts, color palettes, or 3D visualizations for client presentations, the final execution and interpersonal communication with clients remain fundamentally human-centric.

title: フードコーディネーター
rationale: The core tasks of menu development, recipe creation, and marketing planning are highly susceptible to AI-driven generative design and data analysis. However, the role remains anchored by physical requirements such as food styling for photography, hands-on cooking instruction, and the high-level interpersonal coordination needed between chefs, architects, and business owners.

title: ブックデザイナー
rationale: The core tasks of book design—layout, typography, and visual imagery—are increasingly being automated by generative AI and advanced design software. While high-level creative direction and physical material selection (paper texture) remain human-led, the digital nature of the workflow and the rise of AI-generated covers significantly reduce the time required per project, leading to major industry restructuring.

title: ブロックチェーン・エンジニア
rationale: As a purely digital occupation focused on software development and system architecture, core tasks like writing smart contracts and coding applications are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and augmentation. While high-level architectural decisions and security auditing remain critical, AI tools are already significantly accelerating code generation and bug detection in the blockchain domain.

title: プラント設計技術者
rationale: The core tasks of plant design—including simulation, CAD modeling, and technical documentation—are highly digital and increasingly enhanced by AI-driven generative design and automated error-checking. While the role requires physical site visits for inspections and commissioning, the vast majority of the engineering and procurement workflow is susceptible to significant productivity gains through AI integration.

title: プログラマー
rationale: The core tasks of a programmer—writing code, debugging, and unit testing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level architectural design and complex problem-solving still require human oversight, AI tools are already automating routine coding tasks and documentation, significantly reducing the number of entry-level programmers needed to complete a project.

title: プロジェクトマネージャ（IT）
rationale: IT Project Management is highly digital and data-driven, allowing AI to automate routine progress tracking, resource allocation, and documentation. While AI significantly enhances productivity in risk assessment and reporting, the role remains 'High' rather than 'Very High' because it requires complex human-to-human negotiation, conflict resolution, and stakeholder management within Japan's specific corporate hierarchies.

title: ヘルプデスク（IT）
rationale: The core of this role involves processing technical inquiries and troubleshooting via digital channels, tasks that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation through LLM-powered chatbots and automated diagnostic tools. While some physical tasks like hardware setup and on-site repairs remain, the majority of the knowledge-based support and documentation work is being rapidly restructured by AI in Japan's labor-strapped IT sector.

title: 下水道管路施設の点検・調査
rationale: The core of the job requires physical presence for site setup, equipment deployment, and manual inspection in unpredictable underground environments. However, AI is significantly impacting the 'investigation' phase through automated image recognition for defect detection in camera footage and predictive maintenance modeling, which reduces the manual labor required for data analysis and reporting.

title: 不動産鑑定士
rationale: Real estate appraisal involves heavy data analysis of market trends, comparable transactions, and legal regulations, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and predictive modeling. While physical site inspections and high-stakes consulting provide a buffer, the core task of generating appraisal reports is becoming significantly more efficient through AI, likely reducing the total labor hours required per valuation.

title: 中学校教員
rationale: AI will significantly automate lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, which currently contribute to the heavy workload of Japanese teachers. However, the core responsibilities of emotional guidance (seito-shido), managing complex interpersonal dynamics like bullying, and physical supervision of club activities (bukatsu) remain deeply human-centric and resistant to full automation.

title: 中小企業診断士
rationale: The core tasks of analyzing financial statements, market data, and drafting diagnostic reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires high-level human judgment and interpersonal trust-building with SME owners, AI significantly reduces the time needed for data processing and administrative support, fundamentally reshaping how consultants deliver value.

title: 人事コンサルタント
rationale: HR consultants perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, report writing, and policy design, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. While the role requires significant human-to-human interaction, negotiation, and cultural sensitivity within Japanese corporate environments, AI can now automate the bulk of the research, drafting, and diagnostic phases of a project.

title: 作業環境測定士
rationale: The core of the job requires physical presence at industrial sites for sampling and equipment setup, which acts as a barrier to full automation. While AI can significantly streamline data analysis, report generation, and the formulation of improvement proposals, the legal requirement for certified human oversight and the need for site-specific communication with factory managers keep exposure moderate.

title: 作業療法士（OT）
rationale: Occupational therapy is fundamentally a hands-on, interpersonal profession requiring physical manipulation and real-time observation of patients in unpredictable environments. While AI can significantly assist in administrative tasks, diagnostic analysis, and personalized program generation, the core execution of therapy and the emotional support required for rehabilitation remain human-centric.

title: 保健師
rationale: Public health nurses (Hokenshi) perform a mix of data-driven health planning and high-touch interpersonal work, such as home visits and counseling. While AI can significantly automate health data analysis, policy planning, and routine health guidance, the core requirements of physical presence, community trust, and complex emotional support in Japan's aging society provide a strong barrier to full automation.

title: 保育士
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical care, emotional support, and real-time supervision of children in an unpredictable environment, which are tasks highly resistant to AI. While AI will significantly reduce the administrative burden of creating lesson plans, writing reports, and communicating with parents, the fundamental human-to-human interaction and safety responsibilities remain essential.

title: 保育補助者
rationale: This role is primarily physical and interpersonal, involving hands-on childcare, cleaning, and safety monitoring in an unpredictable environment. While AI may assist with administrative tasks or health monitoring sensors, the core duties require human presence and physical dexterity that are difficult to automate, especially given Japan's strict safety standards and cultural emphasis on human-to-human care for children.

title: 児童指導員
rationale: The core of this role involves physical childcare, emotional support, and building trust-based relationships with children in sensitive environments, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like documentation, reporting, and personalized learning plans, the high requirement for real-time human intervention and physical presence in a 24-hour care setting keeps exposure low.

title: 公認会計士
rationale: Certified Public Accountants (CPAs) in Japan perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly data-driven and digital, making core tasks like auditing, tax preparation, and financial analysis highly susceptible to AI automation. While AI can handle massive data verification and anomaly detection, the role remains 'High Exposure' rather than 'Very High' because it still requires significant professional judgment, ethical oversight, and face-to-face consulting with corporate management.

title: 内科医
rationale: Internal medicine is highly exposed to AI in diagnostic data analysis, medical imaging, and personalized treatment planning, which will significantly augment physician productivity. However, the core of the role requires physical examinations (palpation, auscultation), complex human-to-human communication for patient empathy, and legal accountability, which act as significant barriers to full automation in Japan's regulatory environment.

title: 分析化学技術者
rationale: While the physical preparation of samples (cutting, dissolving, grinding) and laboratory safety protocols require human presence, the core value of the job is shifting toward data interpretation and report generation. AI is highly effective at analyzing complex instrument outputs, automating routine chemical calculations, and ensuring regulatory compliance, which will significantly increase the productivity of each technician and automate the more routine aspects of the role.

title: 助産師
rationale: Midwifery requires intense physical presence, manual dexterity for delivery assistance, and high-stakes emotional support that AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist in fetal monitoring, risk prediction, and administrative documentation, the core legal and clinical responsibility for a safe delivery remains a human-centric task in Japan's regulated medical environment.

title: 労働基準監督官
rationale: The role involves significant knowledge work, such as legal analysis, document review, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the core functions require physical on-site inspections, judicial police powers (interrogations/arrests), and complex human-to-human negotiations that necessitate physical presence and high-level human judgment.

title: 動物看護
rationale: The core of the job involves physical tasks like animal restraint, surgical assistance, and facility maintenance that AI cannot perform. However, AI will significantly impact peripheral tasks such as medical record management, diagnostic image analysis assistance, and providing nutritional or behavioral advice to pet owners.

title: 動画制作
rationale: Video production is a digital-first occupation where generative AI is rapidly automating core tasks such as scriptwriting, video editing, color grading, and even the generation of high-quality b-roll or background music. While high-level creative direction and client relationship management remain human-centric, the technical barriers and labor hours required for production are decreasing significantly, leading to major industry restructuring.

title: 医学研究者
rationale: Medical researchers are highly exposed as AI accelerates core tasks like literature review, hypothesis generation, and complex data analysis (e.g., genomics and proteomics). While physical lab experiments and ethical oversight remain human-centric, AI-driven 'dry labs' and automated discovery tools are significantly increasing individual productivity and reshaping the research workflow.

title: 医療機器開発技術者
rationale: The core tasks of medical device design, CAD modeling, and simulation (CAE) are highly digital and increasingly enhanced by AI-driven generative design and automated testing. While strict PMDA regulations and the need for physical prototyping and clinical collaboration provide a buffer, AI will significantly streamline the heavy documentation and regulatory compliance workloads characteristic of the Japanese medical industry.

title: 半導体技術者
rationale: Semiconductor engineering is heavily reliant on digital design (EDA tools), simulation, and complex data analysis for yield improvement, all of which are being transformed by AI to accelerate development cycles. While process engineers still require physical presence in cleanrooms and hardware interaction, the core intellectual work of circuit design and manufacturing optimization is highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.

title: 原子力技術者
rationale: Nuclear engineers rely heavily on complex simulations, data analysis, and safety modeling, all of which are being significantly enhanced by AI and machine learning. While physical inspections and high-stakes regulatory oversight require human presence and accountability, the core design, monitoring, and predictive maintenance tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.

title: 司法書士
rationale: The core tasks of a Judicial Scrivener (Shiho-shoshi)—drafting legal documents, processing registrations, and analyzing statutes—are highly digital and rule-based, making them prime candidates for AI automation. While Japan's specific administrative procedures and the need for human verification in real estate transactions provide some buffer, the shift toward digital government (e-Gov) and AI-driven document generation will significantly reduce the manual labor required per case.

title: 商業カメラマン
rationale: While physical presence and lighting setup are still required for on-site shoots, generative AI is rapidly automating post-production, retouching, and even the creation of commercial backgrounds. Furthermore, AI-generated imagery is increasingly replacing the need for traditional stock photography and some product shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human photographers in the commercial sector.

title: 図書編集者
rationale: The core tasks of manuscript editing, proofreading, layout planning, and market analysis are highly digital and increasingly automatable with LLMs and generative AI. While human relationship management with authors remains critical in the Japanese context, the technical production and administrative oversight of book creation are facing significant restructuring due to AI's ability to process text and design.

title: 図書館司書
rationale: Librarianship involves significant information processing, cataloging, and research assistance, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and enhanced search capabilities. While physical tasks like book handling and community-facing roles provide some insulation, the core functions of information retrieval and data management are increasingly being reshaped by AI in Japan's digital-forward library systems.

title: 国際協力専門家
rationale: The role involves heavy knowledge work, data analysis, and policy formulation which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the core requirements of high-level diplomacy, cross-cultural empathy, and on-the-ground physical presence in developing nations create a significant human-centric barrier to full automation.

title: 土地家屋調査士
rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical fieldwork, including on-site surveying and boundary verification with neighbors, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, the office-based tasks—calculating measurements, drafting technical drawings, and preparing legal registration documents—are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.

title: 土木・建築工学研究者
rationale: This role involves heavy digital work including structural modeling, data analysis, and technical writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. While physical experimentation and site visits provide a buffer, AI is rapidly transforming the core research tasks of simulation, material discovery, and logical synthesis in civil engineering.

title: 土木施工管理技術者
rationale: This role involves a significant amount of information processing, such as planning, scheduling, and regulatory compliance, which AI can streamline through automated site monitoring and resource optimization. However, the core of the job requires physical presence at unpredictable construction sites, real-time problem-solving for physical hazards, and high-level human coordination with diverse stakeholders, creating a strong barrier to full automation.

title: 土木設計技術者
rationale: The core tasks of civil design—structural analysis, CAD drafting, and cost estimation—are increasingly automated by AI and advanced software, significantly boosting productivity. While site visits and stakeholder negotiations provide a physical and interpersonal buffer, the transition toward digital twins and AI-driven generative design places this role in a high-exposure category.

title: 報道カメラマン
rationale: While AI can automate image editing, captioning, and even generate synthetic visuals, the core of photojournalism requires physical presence at unpredictable locations like disaster zones or crime scenes. However, exposure is moderate because AI-driven drones and automated cameras are increasingly used for coverage, and generative AI poses a significant threat to the market value of authentic editorial photography.

title: 声優
rationale: Voice acting is highly exposed because AI voice synthesis and 'cloning' can now replicate specific timbres and emotional nuances with high fidelity, directly threatening dubbing, narration, and NPC dialogue in games. While top-tier creative performances and 'idol' status provide some protection through human brand value, the vast majority of routine voice work is fundamentally digital output that is increasingly replaceable by generative AI.

title: 外科医
rationale: Surgeons face moderate exposure because AI is revolutionizing diagnostic imaging, surgical planning, and robotic-assisted precision. However, the core physical act of surgery in a dynamic biological environment and the high-stakes interpersonal responsibility for patient life provide a significant buffer against full automation.

title: 大学・短期大学教員
rationale: University faculty face high exposure as AI significantly augments core tasks like literature review, data analysis, and drafting research papers, while also transforming pedagogy through AI-driven personalized learning. While the 'human-in-the-loop' remains essential for high-level judgment, mentorship, and committee governance, the digital nature of their output and Japan's shrinking student population will likely lead to major structural shifts and increased productivity expectations per researcher.

title: 太陽光発電の設計・施工
rationale: The design phase is highly susceptible to AI optimization for electrical and structural layouts based on terrain and regulations. However, the construction management (施工) aspect requires physical site visits, real-time problem-solving in unpredictable environments, and human-to-human coordination, which limits total automation.

title: 学童保育指導員
rationale: The core of this role involves physical supervision, emotional support, and real-time behavioral management of children, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While AI can streamline administrative tasks like attendance tracking, reporting, and activity planning, the fundamental requirement for human presence and trust-based relationships in a physical environment keeps exposure low.

title: 学習塾教師
rationale: Core tasks like curriculum design, problem creation, and test grading are highly susceptible to AI automation, while AI-driven e-learning platforms are already restructuring the industry. However, the role retains a significant human element through emotional support, student motivation, and complex parent-teacher consultations, which are critical in Japan's competitive education market.

title: 学芸員
rationale: Curators perform high-level knowledge work including research, cataloging, and exhibition planning, all of which are significantly enhanced by AI's ability to analyze historical data and generate educational content. However, the role remains grounded in the physical management of artifacts, field research, and the human-centric task of cultural storytelling, which limits full automation.

title: 宇宙開発技術者
rationale: Space development engineering is a high-level knowledge profession heavily reliant on digital tools for simulation, CAD design, and data analysis, all of which are being significantly enhanced by AI. While the role requires critical human judgment, complex stakeholder management, and oversight of physical manufacturing/launch operations, AI is becoming indispensable for processing satellite data and optimizing rocket trajectories. In Japan, the push for 'Space Transformation' (SX) and labor shortages in high-tech sectors are accelerating the integration of AI into these engineering workflows.

title: 家庭裁判所調査官
rationale: The role involves significant knowledge work, including psychological analysis and report writing, which AI can streamline through data synthesis and drafting. However, the core of the job requires high-stakes human interaction, such as interviewing minors and observing family dynamics, which involves emotional intelligence and physical presence that AI cannot currently replicate in a judicial context.

title: 専門学校教員
rationale: Vocational school teachers face high exposure because AI can automate curriculum development, lecture content creation, and technical grading, especially in digital fields like IT or design. However, the role remains anchored by the need for hands-on practical training, career counseling, and navigating Japan's specific industry certification requirements which require human mentorship.

title: 小学校教員
rationale: AI significantly impacts the knowledge-work aspects of teaching, such as lesson planning, grading, and creating personalized learning materials, which helps alleviate the heavy administrative burden in Japan. However, the core of the job involves physical supervision, emotional support, and character building for young children, which requires a human presence that AI cannot replace.

title: 幼稚園教員
rationale: While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like lesson planning, documentation, and parent communication, the core of the job involves physical supervision and complex socio-emotional development of young children. Japan's cultural emphasis on human-led early childhood education and the unpredictable physical nature of managing toddlers create high barriers to full automation.

title: 広告ディレクター
rationale: The core outputs of this role—strategy, copywriting, and visual design—are being revolutionized by generative AI, allowing for rapid prototyping and content creation. While the human-centric aspects of client management and team leadership remain, the technical production and creative ideation processes are highly digital and increasingly automated, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for large traditional teams.

title: 広告デザイナー
rationale: The core output of an advertising designer—visual layouts, sketches, and final graphics—is entirely digital and directly aligns with the capabilities of generative AI. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, AI tools can now automate the generation of drafts, image editing, and variations, significantly reducing the labor hours required per project.

title: 広報コンサルタント
rationale: The core outputs of a PR consultant—strategy documents, press releases, media monitoring, and social media content—are fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI generation and analysis. While high-level crisis management and physical event coordination require human judgment and presence, AI significantly automates the research, drafting, and data-driven planning that comprise the bulk of the workload.

title: 建築施工管理技術者
rationale: AI significantly automates the digital aspects of this role, such as scheduling, cost estimation, and safety monitoring via computer vision. However, the core requirement for physical site inspections, real-time human leadership of diverse subcontractors, and navigating unpredictable physical environments in Japan's complex construction sector provides a buffer against full automation.

title: 建築設計技術者
rationale: The core work is digital, involving CAD/BIM software and complex data analysis that AI is increasingly capable of automating, such as structural calculations and regulatory compliance checks. While human judgment is still required for aesthetic design, client negotiations, and on-site supervision, AI will significantly reduce the time needed for technical drafting and routine planning tasks.

title: 弁理士
rationale: Patent attorneys (Benrishi) perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis of technical documents and the drafting of complex legal applications. Generative AI is exceptionally capable of automating prior art searches, summarizing technical specifications, and drafting initial patent claims, which significantly increases productivity and reduces the billable hours required for routine filings. While high-stakes litigation and strategic consulting still require human judgment, the core document-heavy workflow is highly exposed to AI-driven restructuring.

title: 弁護士
rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly augmented by AI in areas like legal research, document review, and contract drafting, which significantly boosts productivity. While the core functions of courtroom advocacy, complex negotiation, and ethical judgment require human presence and empathy, the digital nature of legal documentation makes a large portion of the workload highly susceptible to AI integration.

title: 情報工学研究者
rationale: Information engineering researchers work entirely in digital domains—coding, designing architectures, and writing papers—where AI tools like LLMs and automated theorem provers are already drastically accelerating output. While high-level original research requires human creativity, the core tasks of literature review, simulation, and software prototyping are highly susceptible to AI-driven restructuring.

title: 手話通訳者
rationale: Computer vision and AI translation models are rapidly advancing in their ability to both recognize sign language and generate realistic avatars for signing, making the core task of translation highly susceptible to automation. However, the role remains 'high' rather than 'very high' because it often requires physical presence in sensitive environments (medical, legal, social services) and deep empathy to navigate the diverse linguistic backgrounds and emotional needs of the hearing-impaired community.

title: 探偵
rationale: While AI significantly enhances digital tasks like report writing, image enhancement, and data-driven background checks, the core of the job remains physical surveillance (tailing and stakeouts) in unpredictable environments. Japan's strict privacy laws and the need for legally admissible physical evidence ensure that human presence and real-time judgment remain essential, though AI tools will increase individual investigator productivity.

title: 放送ディレクター
rationale: While the role requires high-level human leadership and on-site coordination of staff and talent, the core creative outputs—scripting, storyboarding, and video editing—are being heavily disrupted by generative AI. AI tools significantly automate the labor-intensive post-production and planning phases, though the final 'human touch' and real-time directorial judgment in unpredictable studio/location settings remain essential.

title: 放送記者
rationale: Broadcast journalism involves heavy digital information processing, including scriptwriting, video editing, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical presence for on-site reporting and human-to-human interviewing remain essential, AI will significantly reduce the labor required for background research, drafting, and post-production editing in the Japanese media landscape.

title: 整形外科医
rationale: Orthopedic surgery involves a high degree of physical intervention, manual dexterity, and real-time tactile feedback during surgery and physical examinations. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays, MRIs) and assist in surgical planning or robotic-assisted procedures, the core requirements of physical presence and complex human-to-human interaction in a clinical setting provide a strong buffer against full automation.

title: 新聞記者
rationale: The core output of a journalist—writing, summarizing, and structuring information—is highly susceptible to AI automation, particularly for routine reporting and digital editions. While physical presence for interviews and investigative 'boots-on-the-ground' work provides a buffer, AI significantly increases productivity in drafting and editing, potentially reducing the total number of human journalists required in a shrinking Japanese print market.

title: 日本語教師
rationale: Core tasks like lesson planning, material creation, and automated essay grading are highly susceptible to AI, which is already listed as a tool in the job description. While the interpersonal aspect of classroom management and cultural integration remains important, AI-driven personalized learning and real-time translation tools will significantly restructure how language instruction is delivered and assessed.

title: 映像編集者
rationale: Video editing is a fundamentally digital occupation where AI is rapidly automating core tasks such as color grading, object removal, transcription-based cutting, and even creative assembly. While high-level artistic direction remains human-led, the productivity gains from AI mean fewer editors are needed to produce the same volume of content, and entry-level 'assistant' tasks are being heavily disrupted.

title: 林業技術者
rationale: While core tasks involve physical field surveys and on-site supervision in unpredictable natural environments, AI and drones are significantly transforming the data collection, forest inventory analysis, and management planning aspects of the role. Japan's labor shortage is accelerating the adoption of smart forestry technologies, but the need for human judgment in ecological conservation and interpersonal coordination with forest owners remains high.

title: 柔道整復師
rationale: The core of the job involves manual therapy (shugi), physical manipulation, and tactile diagnosis (palpation) that require physical presence and human touch. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like insurance billing (recept) and provide diagnostic support or treatment planning, the physical execution of resetting bones and applying bandages remains highly resistant to automation.

title: 栄養士
rationale: Core digital tasks like meal planning, nutritional calculation, and inventory management are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role remains significant in Japan's healthcare and elderly care sectors, requiring physical site inspections, coordination with kitchen staff, and empathetic face-to-face nutritional counseling that AI cannot fully replace.

title: 植物工場の研究開発
rationale: This role involves high-level knowledge work such as data analysis of environmental variables, genetic research, and system optimization, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven acceleration. While physical 'hands-on' verification in the factory remains necessary, the core tasks of experimental design, simulation, and predictive modeling for crop yields are increasingly being automated by AI and machine learning.

title: 植物工場の設計、施工
rationale: The design phase is highly digital and susceptible to AI-driven CAD optimization, environmental simulations, and automated resource planning. However, the role also involves physical construction management, site inspections, and manual coordination with contractors, which provide a buffer against full automation in the Japanese labor market.

title: 検察事務官
rationale: The role involves extensive documentation, evidence analysis, and administrative processing (事務手続), all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains. While tasks like suspect interrogation and victim support require human empathy and legal judgment, the core output of drafting legal documents and managing case records is increasingly being transformed by AI tools.

title: 検察官
rationale: Prosecutors deal with vast amounts of digital and written evidence, where AI can significantly accelerate document review, case law research, and evidence synthesis. However, the core responsibilities—interrogating suspects, making discretionary decisions on whether to prosecute (indictment), and performing in-court litigation—require high-level human judgment, ethical considerations, and interpersonal intuition that are resistant to full automation.

title: 検疫官（看護師）
rationale: The role combines physical tasks like visual inspections, vaccinations, and pest collection with significant administrative and information-processing duties. While AI can automate document screening, translation, and real-time infection data analysis, the core requirement for physical presence at ports and human-to-human clinical judgment limits full automation.

title: 機械設計技術者
rationale: Mechanical design is heavily digital, relying on CAD, CAE, and simulation tools where AI is rapidly automating generative design, optimization, and error-checking. While physical prototyping and cross-departmental coordination remain essential, AI significantly reduces the time required for technical drafting and complex calculations, increasing individual productivity and shifting the role toward high-level oversight and requirements definition.

title: 歯科助手
rationale: The administrative and clerical components (reception, billing, and scheduling) are highly susceptible to AI automation and self-service systems. However, the core physical tasks—such as preparing instruments, disinfecting equipment, and providing real-time chairside assistance—require physical presence and manual dexterity that AI cannot yet replace in a clinical setting.

title: 歯科医師
rationale: Dentistry requires high-precision manual dexterity and physical intervention in a complex, unpredictable biological environment, which remains difficult to automate. However, AI is significantly impacting the profession through advanced diagnostic imaging (AI-driven X-ray/CT analysis), treatment planning for orthodontics, and administrative tasks, making it a mix of physical skill and AI-assisted knowledge work.

title: 歯科技工士
rationale: The profession is undergoing a massive digital shift as CAD/CAM systems and 3D printing replace traditional manual molding and casting. While the final product is physical, the core work of designing prosthetics is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and generative design, though final quality control and intricate finishing still require human expertise.

title: 歯科衛生士
rationale: The core tasks of a dental hygienist involve highly precise manual procedures in the human oral cavity and real-time interpersonal health guidance, which are resistant to AI automation. While AI will enhance diagnostic imaging and administrative scheduling, the physical nature of cleaning teeth and assisting in surgeries ensures the role remains human-centric in the Japanese healthcare context.

title: 気象予報士
rationale: The core task of analyzing vast datasets and numerical models to predict weather is highly susceptible to AI and machine learning, which can process complex variables more efficiently than humans. While legal requirements in Japan mandate certified meteorologists for forecasting services and human 'weather casters' remain popular for communication, the technical analytical work is undergoing massive restructuring due to AI-driven precision.

title: 水産技術者
rationale: This role involves significant data analysis, resource modeling, and research design which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization. However, the requirement for physical field surveys, laboratory experiments, and direct technical guidance to fishermen provides a buffer against full automation.

title: 治験コーディネーター
rationale: The role involves heavy administrative and data-oriented tasks, such as creating case reports and scheduling, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and streamlining. However, the core requirement for empathetic patient communication, ethical oversight, and complex coordination between doctors and pharmaceutical companies provides a significant human-centric buffer that AI cannot fully replace.

title: 法務技官（心理）（矯正心理専門職）
rationale: The role involves significant digital-friendly tasks like psychological assessment, report writing (鑑別結果通知書), and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core functions require high-stakes human interaction, clinical empathy, and physical presence within correctional facilities, creating a strong barrier to full automation in the Japanese legal and penal system.

title: 法務教官
rationale: The core of this role involves high-stakes human interaction, behavioral observation, and 24-hour physical supervision within correctional facilities, which are resistant to AI replacement. However, AI can significantly assist in analyzing psychological data, drafting rehabilitation reports, and providing personalized educational content (教科指導), leading to moderate exposure in the administrative and diagnostic aspects of the job.

title: 測量士
rationale: Surveying is seeing a significant shift as AI, drones, and GIS automate high-precision data collection and complex spatial calculations, reducing the manual labor required for mapping. However, the role remains anchored by the physical necessity of visiting sites for 'sumidashi' (marking) and navigating unpredictable outdoor environments, which prevents full automation.

title: 特別支援学校教員、特別支援学級教員
rationale: While AI can significantly assist with administrative tasks, drafting individualized education plans (IEPs), and creating customized digital learning materials, the core of the job involves high-intensity physical care and complex emotional interaction. The necessity for real-time human intervention in unpredictable environments and the specialized physical support required for students with disabilities create a strong barrier to full AI replacement.

title: 特許審査官
rationale: The core tasks of a patent examiner—searching global databases, analyzing technical documents, and comparing claims against prior art—are fundamentally digital and information-intensive, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While final legal judgment and complex negotiations require human accountability, AI can already perform high-speed prior art searches and draft preliminary assessments, significantly reducing the human labor required per application.

title: 犬訓練士
rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical interaction with animals in unpredictable environments and manual labor such as feeding and health management. While AI might assist in peripheral tasks like scheduling, client communication, or analyzing dog behavior via video, the primary training and care functions require a physical human presence that cannot be digitized.

title: 独立系ファイナンシャル・アドバイザー（IFA）
rationale: IFA work is heavily data-driven, involving financial analysis, portfolio optimization, and market monitoring—tasks where AI excels and can automate significant portions of the workflow. While the 'human-to-human' trust and life-planning aspects provide a buffer, AI-driven robo-advisors and personalized LLM financial assistants will increasingly handle the routine technical advice and administrative execution, shifting the human role toward high-level emotional coaching and complex multi-disciplinary coordination.

title: 獣医師
rationale: AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays, CT scans) and data-driven treatment planning, particularly in biomedical research and public health monitoring. However, the core of the job involves physical examinations, complex surgeries, and manual procedures on unpredictable animals, alongside high-stakes communication with owners, which limits full automation.

title: 理学療法士（PT）
rationale: Physical therapy requires significant manual intervention, real-time physical adjustments, and empathetic human interaction, which are difficult to automate. However, AI will highly impact the 'knowledge' components of the job, such as analyzing patient data to create optimized recovery programs and using computer vision to track progress more accurately than the human eye. In Japan's aging society, AI-driven diagnostic tools and robotic exoskeletons will likely become standard assistants, increasing per-worker productivity without replacing the core physical role.

title: 生産・品質管理技術者
rationale: This role involves heavy data analysis, scheduling, and cross-departmental coordination, all of which are being revolutionized by AI-driven supply chain optimization and predictive quality control. While it requires human judgment for inter-departmental negotiations and physical site inspections, the core tasks of planning and monitoring are increasingly automated in Japan's advanced manufacturing sector.

title: 産婦人科医
rationale: AI will significantly impact diagnostic tasks, such as analyzing fetal ultrasounds and identifying gynecological tumors, while also streamlining administrative documentation. However, the core of the role involves high-stakes physical procedures (surgery and delivery) and complex interpersonal support that are difficult to automate, especially given Japan's strict medical regulations and the cultural importance of human presence during childbirth.

title: 産業医
rationale: AI can significantly assist in analyzing health checkup data, identifying high-risk employees, and ensuring regulatory compliance, which are core parts of the role. However, the job requires high-level human judgment, mediation between management and labor, and physical workplace inspections (職場巡視) that are difficult to fully automate in Japan's complex corporate environments.

title: 産業廃棄物処理技術者
rationale: This role combines digital knowledge work, such as data analysis, regulatory compliance, and process planning, with physical tasks like on-site inspections and laboratory analysis of hazardous materials. While AI can significantly optimize waste processing algorithms and automate reporting, the requirement for physical presence in unpredictable industrial environments and the high stakes of handling toxic substances provide a buffer against full automation.

title: 産業用ロボットの設置・設定
rationale: While the physical installation and wiring of robots require manual presence, the core tasks of system design, programming, and simulation are increasingly automated by AI-driven 'no-code' tools and digital twins. In Japan's labor-short market, AI is being aggressively integrated into the SIer workflow to speed up robot deployment, shifting the human role toward high-level system architecture and client communication.

title: 産業用ロボット開発技術者
rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core outputs—software coding, CAD design, and data analysis—are digital tasks where AI is rapidly advancing. While the job requires physical testing and hardware integration, AI is becoming a primary tool for optimizing robot trajectories, simulating mechanical stress, and developing the 'Physical AI' brains that govern modern industrial automation.

title: 畜産技術者
rationale: This role combines high-level knowledge work, such as biotechnology research and data analysis for breeding, with essential physical tasks like animal handling and onsite farm inspections. While AI can significantly optimize feed formulations, disease diagnostics, and environmental monitoring, the requirement for physical presence in unpredictable environments and hands-on technical guidance limits full automation.

title: 看護助手
rationale: The core tasks involve physical labor and direct patient care, such as bathing, feeding, and moving patients, which are difficult for AI to automate in unpredictable hospital environments. While AI can streamline peripheral administrative tasks like inventory management and documentation, the high demand for human-to-human care in Japan's aging society ensures the role remains fundamentally physical and interpersonal.

title: 看護師
rationale: Nursing involves a high degree of physical tasks, bedside care, and real-time human interaction that AI cannot replicate. While AI will significantly assist in diagnostics, patient monitoring, and administrative documentation, the core responsibilities of physical treatment and emotional support remain human-centric, especially given Japan's regulatory environment and aging population.

title: 知的財産コーディネーター
rationale: This role is primarily digital and knowledge-based, involving patent searches, document analysis, and legal strategy, all of which are being heavily augmented by LLMs and specialized AI search tools. While it requires high-level human coordination between researchers and lawyers, AI can now automate the core technical tasks of prior art research, drafting initial claims, and summarizing complex technical disclosures.

title: 知的財産サーチャー
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, revolving around the collection, analysis, and synthesis of vast amounts of text-based data (patents and technical literature). LLMs and specialized AI tools are exceptionally proficient at drafting search queries, summarizing complex technical documents, and identifying prior art, which directly automates the core value proposition of a searcher.

title: 社会保険労務士
rationale: The core tasks of a Sharoushi—preparing complex legal documents, filing administrative procedures, and calculating insurance/pensions—are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime targets for AI automation. While high-level labor consulting and dispute resolution require human judgment, the vast majority of routine compliance and filing work is being rapidly streamlined by AI and government e-portal integrations.

title: 社会学研究者
rationale: Sociologists rely heavily on data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing, all of which are being significantly enhanced by LLMs and advanced statistical AI. While the core tasks of identifying social problems and conducting qualitative interviews require human empathy and sociological imagination, AI can automate the heavy lifting of coding qualitative data and processing large-scale quantitative datasets.

title: 社会教育主事
rationale: The role involves significant knowledge work such as planning educational programs, researching regional issues, and administrative reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the core requirements of community coordination, interpersonal relationship management with local organizations (PTA, youth groups), and hands-on guidance provide a buffer against full automation.

title: 科学捜査研究所鑑定技術職員
rationale: The role involves heavy data analysis, pattern recognition, and image/voice processing, all of which are being revolutionized by AI to increase accuracy and speed. While physical lab work and legal accountability provide a buffer, the core tasks of DNA matching, handwriting analysis, and digital forensics are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and enhancement.

title: 税理士
rationale: The core tasks of tax preparation, document creation, and routine accounting are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, especially with Japan's push for e-Tax and automated accounting software. While high-level tax strategy and representation in audits require human judgment and trust, the vast majority of the workload is data-driven and increasingly handled by sophisticated AI models, leading to significant industry restructuring.

title: 精密機器技術者
rationale: The core tasks of conceptual design, CAD modeling, and simulation (CAE) are increasingly automated by generative design and AI-driven optimization tools. While physical prototyping and cross-departmental coordination provide a buffer, the shift toward digital twins and automated quality control significantly increases the productivity and exposure of this role.

title: 精神科医
rationale: Psychiatry involves significant knowledge work such as diagnosis and pharmacological analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven decision support and automation. However, the core of the profession relies on nuanced human-to-human interaction, empathy, and the legal authority to perform involuntary admissions, which remain difficult to automate in Japan's regulatory and cultural landscape.

title: 細胞検査士
rationale: The core task of a cytotechnologist—screening microscope slides for abnormal cells—is a high-precision pattern recognition task that is highly susceptible to AI-driven digital pathology and computer-aided detection (CAD). While physical sample preparation remains, the shift toward digital imaging allows AI to perform the primary screening and triage of cases, significantly increasing productivity and reducing the number of human specialists required for routine diagnostics.

title: 経営コンサルタント
rationale: Management consulting involves heavy data analysis, report generation, and strategic modeling, all of which are being significantly accelerated by generative AI. While high-level stakeholder management and organizational change require human empathy and trust, the 'back-office' production of deliverables and research is highly exposed to automation, leading to massive productivity gains and potential workforce restructuring.

title: 義肢装具士
